The beef resembled shoe leather, and the veggies had lost all their vibrant colors and crispness.Our server, bless his heart, tried his best to salvage the experience. He was genuinely kind and polite, but unfortunately, the kitchen's shortcomings were beyond his control. Even the presentation of the dishes seemed lackluster, with sloppy plating and a messy drizzle of sauce that did little to enhance the overall appearance.As we pushed through the mediocre meal, we couldn't help but notice the lack of attention from the staff. Our water glasses remained unfilled for most of the evening, and it took an excruciatingly long time to get their attention when we needed something. It felt as though they were understaffed, struggling to keep up with the busy evening crowd. To top it all off, when we finally made it to the dessert stage, the options were disappointingly limited. It seemed like they had run out of ideas or perhaps didn't prioritize that part of the menu. We settled for a generic slice of chocolate cake, but even that failed to impress.

I woke up to a sterile, white room, surrounded by people in white lab coats. Confusion clouded my mind as I tried to grasp where I was and what was happening. A doctor approached me with a solemn expression on his face. "Welcome back," he said, his voice tinged with a mix of excitement and sorrow.I blinked, trying to process his words. "Back? Where am I? What happened?" I stammered, the rush of questions filling my mind.The doctor sighed, his gaze locking with mine. "You've been brought back from the dead," he said, his voice heavy with the weight of the revelation. "You're the first successful resurrection."A wave of disbelief washed over me. Brought back from the dead? It seemed impossible, like something out of a science fiction novel. I scanned the room, noticing the intricate medical equipment and the air of urgency that hung in the air."But... why?" I managed to utter, my voice trembling.The doctor explained the nature of the procedure, the countless hours of research and experimentation. He spoke of a breakthrough that defied all expectations but came with a devastating caveat – I would only have a few precious hours of life before it would slip away once more.As the reality sank in, a mix of emotions surged through me. Gratitude for a second chance at life mingled with an overwhelming sense of grief for the inevitable loss that haunted every passing moment."How... how did you bring me back?" I asked, my voice barely above a whisper.The doctor's eyes sparkled with a glimmer of pride. "We utilized groundbreaking technology, a combination of advanced medical procedures and experimental treatments," he explained, his voice tinged with both awe and caution. "It was a risky endeavor, but you defied the odds."I couldn't help but marvel at the sheer audacity of their achievement. The implications were staggering – the boundary between life and death had been challenged, even if only for a fleeting moment.The room fell into a pensive silence.

A statue of Cristiano Ronaldo has been unveiled on the Portuguese island of Madeira, but many football fans are questioning its resemblance to the player. The sculptor, Emanuel Santos, has defended his work, saying it is "a matter of taste" and that Ronaldo himself has been shown the photos and liked what he saw. However, the statue has been met with widespread criticism on social media, with many users suggesting that it does not accurately represent the footballer's likeness. Despite this, Santos has stated that he is "not bound" by the criticism and will continue to work on his art.

It coincides with the first International Dylan Thomas Day in Swansea.The old exercise book, lying in a drawer for decades, was bought by Swansea University for Â£104,500 last December.An exhibition on Thursday will allow a maximum of 200 people to see the book.Prof John Goodby, of Swansea University, described it as the "holy grail" for Thomas scholars and the most exciting discovery related to the poet since his death in 1953.It is thought Thomas left it at the house of his mother-in-law in the 1930s but it was forgotten about.It is one of five notebooks used by Thomas - the other four are in the State University of New York at Buffalo.The notebook will be on display under protective glass but plasma screens will show the whole of its contents.The poet's granddaughter Hannah Ellis said the crossings out and re-writings in the book showed the teenage Thomas's meticulous approach and experimentation with language."It was so important this notebook would be seen by scholars, fans and the general public," she told BBC Wales."The other notebooks had just been left in boxes and it was only during the centenary year they came to Wales and were seen by the public."The exhibition takes place at the university's council chamber and the date coincides with the first performance of Under Milk Wood in New York in 1953.

And what Walnut Street lacks in watering hole quantity, Le Mardi Gras (I've only heard it called Mardi Gras) makes up for in quality. nnIt's a lame clichu00e9 to use Frank Sinatra as a representative for all things 50's and 60's posh, but they do play a lot of his stuff and a man in a double-breasted pinstripe suit and fedora having a Manhattan while waiting to meet his friends after work, or sipping his way through a three-martini lunch, would fit in seamlessly here. nnBefore you settle into a wide booth to enjoy the music and let your eyes adjust to the darkness, order your beverage at the bar (make sure you bring those greenbacks-cash only) because the bartenders (white-haired, male, wearing vests and drying glasses) will not be making their way out to you. Their laissez-faire customer service could be considered inconvenient but I prefer to think of it as adding to the relaxed and cozy vibe. nnDrinks are both stronger and a little more expensive than the average PGH bar. My martini (a cocktail I'm very particular about) was icy goodness. And they have tons of great beers (Guinness, Dogfish Head, Sierra Nevada) on tap. nnThis would be the perfect spot to have a post-shopping-binge cocktail (after spending the day handing over my scratch to America's shiniest corporate retailers I always like to unwind with a drink), except there's smoking allowed inside and I don't want that "ashtray-fresh" smell all over my new goods.

The Ostriker-Vishniac effect, which produces a polarized signature of the cosmic microwave background (CMB), has been utilized to detect galaxy clusters at high redshift. By employing CMB lensing, we can uncover the distribution of matter in these clusters and investigate their properties. Our approach allows for the isolation of the lensed CMB signal, enabling the examination of the cluster's mass profile. This technique has the potential to reveal the presence of galaxy clusters at redshifts beyond what is currently feasible through conventional means. The application of this method will provide vital insights into the evolution and composition of high-redshift galaxy clusters.

Abstract:In this study, we investigate the properties of quantum and classical spins on a spatially distorted Kagome lattice, focusing on their relevance to the mineral compound Volborthite. By employing a combination of theoretical modeling and numerical simulations, we explore the intricate interplay between spatial distortions and spin interactions in this unique lattice structure. Our analysis involves both quantum and classical spin models, allowing us to examine the distinct behaviors exhibited by these two frameworks. We begin by constructing a theoretical framework based on the Heisenberg model, which captures the quantum nature of spins and their interactions on the distorted Kagome lattice. Using advanced techniques such as Dzyaloshinsky-Moriya interactions and post-Newtonian order calculations, we elucidate the influence of geometric distortions on the spin dynamics. Additionally, we extend our analysis to include classical spin models, investigating the effects of thermal fluctuations and anisotropic exchange interactions on the magnetic properties of Volborthite.By conducting extensive Monte Carlo simulations and exact diagonalization techniques, we obtain comprehensive insights into the ground state properties, excitation spectra, and magnetic phase transitions of both quantum and classical spin systems on the distorted Kagome lattice. Furthermore, we compare our results with experimental observations of Volborthite, providing a valuable theoretical framework for understanding the underlying physical mechanisms governing its magnetic behavior.Our findings demonstrate that the spatial distortions in the Kagome lattice play a crucial role in the emergence of exotic magnetic phenomena in Volborthite. The quantum nature of spins manifests in the form of spin liquid behavior and fractionalized excitations, whereas classical spins exhibit a rich variety of ordered and disordered magnetic phases.

We present results of an attempt to include more detailed gas physicsmotivated from hydrodynamical simulations within semi-analytic models (SAM) ofgalaxy formation, focusing on the role that environmental effects play. Themain difference to previous SAMs is that we include 'gravitational' heating ofthe intra-cluster medium (ICM) by the net surplus of gravitational potentialenergy released from gas that has been stripped from infalling satellites.Gravitational heating appears to be an efficient heating source able to preventcooling in environments corresponding to dark matter halos more massive than$\sim 10^{13} $M$_{\odot}$. The energy release by gravitational heating canmatch that by AGN-feedback in massive galaxies and can exceed it in the mostmassive ones. However, there is a fundamental difference in the way the twoprocesses operate. Gravitational heating becomes important at late times, whenthe peak activity of AGNs is already over, and it is very mass dependent. Thismass dependency and time behaviour gives the right trend to recover down-sizingin the star-formation rate of massive galaxies. Abridged...

In a move to protect children and vulnerable individuals, US authorities have taken action against registered sex offenders who were using online gaming accounts to potentially harm others. Hundreds of such accounts have been shut down, denying access to these dangerous individuals. This move comes as a part of a larger effort to tighten security measures and ensure that online spaces remain safe for all users. The initiative has received support from law enforcement agencies and gaming companies, who are committed to protecting their communities. With this step, the US is sending a strong message that online harassment and exploitation will not be tolerated.

The beer selection is fantastic (you had me at Weihenstepher), the service was great, and the food was really, really good. I ordered the Wurst Platte, which, as it's name suggests, is a plate of sausages--three different kinds, to be exact, with a side of kraut and home fries. There was also a perfect dribbling of jaeger sauza to go with it. I've always like kraut, but not loved it. This stuff, I loved. It actually had a slightly sweet flavor to counteract the normal tang of kraut. My wife had a pork schnitzel that was flayed and stuffed with black forest ham and cheese. Nothing better than stuffing pork with pork, my friends. That was served with a side of spaetzle and broccoli that was actually fresh. nnThe old timer walking around playing the accordian was a nice touch and got everyone going. There were a lot of large parties, but we were seated quickly and also served quickly. The location is a little out of the way for a Charlottean, but well worth the trip if you find yourself south of town.

**In a tragic turn of events, a veteran US photojournalist and a translator have lost their lives in Helmand province, situated in southern Afghanistan. The circumstances surrounding their deaths remain undisclosed, adding to the gravity of the situation. Their absence will undoubtedly leave a void in the journalistic community, as they courageously reported on the complexities of the region.**

In a recent legal development, a former teacher at an all girls school in Essex has been acquitted of charges accusing him of sexually touching a girl under the age of 18. The teacher, whose identity has not been disclosed, stood trial for the alleged offense, but the jury returned a verdict of not guilty. The incident in question was said to have occurred during the teacher's tenure at the school, leading to an investigation and subsequent legal proceedings. The defense argued that there was insufficient evidence to support the allegations brought against the accused, and the jury ultimately reached a decision in favor of the defendant. As a result of the acquittal, the teacher has been cleared of all charges and is free from further legal repercussions in relation to this case.

In a tragic incident, a five-year-old boy is fighting for his life after being struck by a car driven by a suspected learner driver. The horrifying incident unfolded when the driver lost control of the vehicle and ran over the helpless child before crashing into a nearby house. The young boy sustained life-threatening injuries upon impact and was immediately rushed to the hospital for emergency medical attention. Witnesses at the scene described the horrifying ordeal, expressing their shock and disbelief at the unfortunate sequence of events. Authorities have initiated an investigation into the incident, focusing on determining the cause of the accident and the qualifications of the driver. The family of the injured child is devastated, and their prayers are with the young boy as he battles for his life. The local community has rallied around the family, offering support and comfort during this unimaginably difficult time.

Dobey, 26, reached the last eight of the Grand Slam of Darts in November and also appeared in the European Championship in 2016.Pipe won their previous meeting 6-4 in Coventry in May."You can't be friends on stage, you've got to want to beat who's in front of you," Dobey told BBC Newcastle."He's a great lad, I've played him before and he's got his own style. Some people don't like the way he plays, he's quite a slow player. I respect him, I'm looking forward to it."Northumbrian Dobey's rise to earning a place at the World Championship is reward for a rapid progression through the professional ranks."I was more into football at an earlier age, but darts is something I'm good at," Dobey added."It's more a natural talent, my dad, grandfather and uncles all played, I've just come on the scene playing five or six years and came along the last year or so."I'm enjoying it and doing well, which is a bonus."

The council quieted down as the meeting began. "Gentlemen, I think you all know why we are here. ", Admiral Gardner began as he turned on a projector. "The Klingons have advanced further then we' ve anticipated and they have taken Midos V and the Proxima colony of the Alpha Centauri system. "A groan fills the room. "However, that' s not the worst of it. We' ve lost Piccard and Kirk ", the admiral added sadly. Immediately the council was led into uproar. A councilman in the back finally quieted them all. "Let him speak, its clear he has a plan to pitch ", he says as they quiet down. "Thank you, councilman. I understand that this is against protocol and even against nature, but the solution I propose is protocol Black Pearl ", says Gardner. A murmur of confusion goes around the room. Gardner clicks a button on his remote, and the first slide to pop up is a centuries old wanted poster of what looked to be a madman. "This, is Captain Jack Sparrow an- ""He' s only a legend! If we wanted tall-tales I would have called my son' s nanny in! "Laughter erupts all around and Gardner patiently waits it out. "I know this sounds insane, but he was a real person. He was also one of the best captains to ever live. We also are able to resurrect him. ", Gardner finishes. A councilman stood up in the front, "Even if we actually believed this, how would a 17th century Caribbean pirate possibly help us with the fight against the might of the Klingons? "It was at this point Gardner smiled, which threw everyone off balance. "The problem is not the problem. The problem is your attitude about the problem. ", he said smugly.

This theater is a beautiful example of the elegance that theaters of the past have, with its opulent chandeliers, plush red seats, and intricate moldings that adorn the walls and ceiling. The moment you step inside, you're transported back in time to an era of glamour and sophistication. The sound of the chandeliers' gentle sway and the soft hum of the audience's conversations create a warm and inviting atmosphere that makes you feel like you're in for a special treat.The stage is spacious and well-lit, with a beautiful proscenium arch that sets the scene for the talented performers who grace it. The acoustics are impressive, with every note and word spoken crystal clear and resonating throughout the theater. The seats are comfortable and have ample legroom, ensuring that you can sit back and enjoy the show without any discomfort.The staff are friendly and attentive, with a keen attention to detail that makes sure every aspect of your visit is a pleasant one. From the ticket takers to the bartenders, everyone is eager to help and ensure that you have a wonderful time. The concessions are reasonably priced, with a variety of snacks and drinks to choose from, and the bathrooms are clean and well-maintained.Overall, this theater is a true gem, a testament to the rich history of theater and the arts. It's a place that feels both classic and modern, timeless and yet always pushing the boundaries of what's possible. Whether you're a seasoned theater-goer or a newcomer to the world of live performances, this theater is sure to leave you in awe and inspire you to return again and again.

**AIG's Former Chief Denied Damages in Lawsuit Against US Government Over 2008 Bailout**:In a recent court ruling, Hank Greenberg, the former head of insurance giant AIG, has been denied any compensation in his legal battle against the US government. The case centered around the controversial 2008 government bailout of AIG, which aimed to stabilize the company during the financial crisis. Greenberg argued that the government's actions during the bailout caused significant losses for him and other shareholders. However, the court dismissed his claims, ruling in favor of the government. This outcome marks a significant defeat for Greenberg, who has consistently maintained that the government overstepped its authority in its handling of AIG.

A large group of us arrived after a show on Friday, April 18. There were 15 of us, and it was 10: 45pm. nnDespite us wandering-in so late, and despite the fact that the crew had mostly cleaned-up for the night (we were the only people there), the staff was *extremely* friendly, accommodating, prompt, and helpful. nn+ It looks exactly the same as I remember it from back in the dayn+ Decent tap selectionn+ INCREDIBLE tap prices - I got a 32oz mug of Lake Louie for $6nn- Cadillac margarita was served warm (despite being on rocks) - not sure how that happened, but it happened twice. n- Cadillac margarita delivered a nice headache the next day. That's unusual, as we normally don't experience one when we have "good" tequila. I'm uncertain what type of tequila they use in the cadillac version, but I suspect it was still closer to "rail"nn+ Good chips and salsa, and they were complimentary, even though we didn't order any other foodnn+ Tons of seating optionsn- Tables/chairs - looking a little dated and some were quite wobblynnAll-in-all, this was a great option for our group, and I couldn't have been any happier than I was with the service. It was really, really outstanding. I'd return for drinks and appetizers again.

**Abstract**This paper studies noncolliding Brownian motion and determinantal processes. We first introduce the basic concepts of noncolliding Brownian motion and determinantal processes. We then discuss some of the main results in this area, including the connection between noncolliding Brownian motion and determinantal processes, and the applications of noncolliding Brownian motion and determinantal processes to other areas of mathematics and physics. Finally, we outline some of the open problems in this area.

I have been by this place many times and was excited to finally make it inside. We were lucky that we went fairly early as a line developed shortly after we were seated at our table. The Rusty Nail is prepared for this in a pretty cool way though as they have a list outside of the restaurant that you can sign into as well as some coffee to partake of while you wait. nnFor my entree I decided to go with one of the specials for the day which was the Rusty Nail omelet while my girlfriend decided on another special which was the a mushroom, spinach and feta junior omelet. Her omelet is self explanatory while mine had in it some salami, cheese and three pieces of sliced pepperoni on top of it. It was readily apparent that the pepperoni was not the cheap kind that you get on most pizzas as it had more flavoring and tasted much better. Both omelets came with sides of home fries and toast for which we both ordered deli rye. The junior omelet tasted good as the sourness of the feta played nicely with earthy mushrooms while the spinach made it feel like I was eating healthy. My omelet was just as good if not better than the junior omelet as the cheese and meats combined to make it seem like I was almost eating a pizza. Some of my hash browns were slightly overcooked and they had too many onions for my taste but we still decent enough to be a side to the main show. nnThe bottom line for me is that I would definitely head back here in the future. Everything that I tasted was good and I would not hesitate to take any of my friends here for a breakfast.

Yes, I called them and asked for a price on ordering a case of import beer. When they called me back with the price, I decilned purchase. I was talking at the same time to another distributor who was doing a lot more leg work for me tracking down the supply chain. When he informed me he could have a case shipped to wherever I bought my beer, I gave him Banksville Beer's name thinking I was giving them some extra business. I don't normally get my beer from there. When I stopped into Banksville to pick it up, a person who I assume was the owner got immediately defensive with me, raised his voice saying "You told me you didn't want it. " I tried to explain what had happened, but he continuously interupted me with a constantly rising voice level barely allowing me to get a word in. He claimed he did not speak to the guy I had spoken to, which was obviously a lie since he referred specifically to "that person you (meaning me) talked to. " I finally had to cut him off and say, "Listen, do you have the case here or not? " He said they did not, so I left. nnI can understand his frustration if what I did caused him any loss or inconveneince, but he did not need to take the majorly unprofessional approach that he did. I honestly had no idea that I did anything wrong, and on the contrary thought I had actually done them a favor. Had he explained the circumstance, it would have been no harm, no foul and lesson learned. And I just would have asked them to order me a case straight out and would have returned for more cases to be orderd in the future. But because of that reaction, I will NEVER step foot in there again under any circumstance. No beer is worth dealing with a world class A-hole like that.

Malcolm Beer made his comments ahead of a Windsor council aviation forum later.He said a third runway north-west of the airport could create the need to use greenbelt land for housing.A Heathrow spokesman said: "There will be little or no need for additional house-building over and above current local authority plans."The housing concern comes after a recent report by the Airport Commission, which stated the Heathrow expansion would create between 47,400 and 112,400 jobs by 2030, which in turn would require an extra 29,800 to 70,800 homes to be created in the surrounding area, including Windsor, Slough and London boroughs.Mr Beer said "anxious" Windsor residents associations would be organising a public meeting in the next 10 days ahead of the commission's public consultation deadline on 3 February.Mr Beer, who is on the Royal Borough of Windsor and Maidenhead Borough aviation forum committee, said: "We have an enormous housing problem in the area."We are having a real problem considering if we have to release greenbelt land for housing, which is an unpopular suggestion."Apart from the noise, the whole area will be urbanised - that's what a lot of people don't realise."The impact will be felt across the Thames Valley - it's commercial greed gone mad."The borough council forum will present its own residents' poll plans after a Heathrow report stated most residents were in favour of expansion.The Heathrow spokesman said the expansion plans would benefit 700,000 existing residents in the area "who are unemployed, under-employed or are commuting out of the area at present".The aviation forum takes place at 19:00 GMT at The Guildhall Chamber, Windsor.

Matthew Collett-Leslie, 14, from Hastings, was first referred to Sussex Child and Adolescent Mental Health Services (Camhs) in November 2014.In May, a psychologist said he showed signs of autism but would need a full assessment, scheduled for 26 November.The NHS trust said there had been a significant rise in referrals.Matthew, who was referred to Camhs by his school, Hailsham Community College, has started his petition on charge.org and written to the prime minister.He said the wait for a possible diagnosis had affected his school and home life."Children do pick on me sometimes because they don't feel the way I am feeling," he said."There is not much support at school because I am not officially diagnosed."His father Joe said Matthew needed help and support at school from a teaching assistant."At home I need the support for myself to deal with the situation," he said.Tom Purser, of the National Autistic Society, said a diagnosis of the condition could help young people understand their behaviour and entitle them to support at home and at school.Sussex Partnership NHS Foundation Trust said: "Assessments for autism involve several stages with a number of different professionals because the symptoms are complex and often confused with other conditions."There has been been a significant rise in the number of referrals to us which has added to the pressures on our services."It is a key national priority to reduce the wait and we are working with other NHS professionals to look at the delays... to see what more can be done."Phil Matthews, principal of Hailsham Community College, confirmed that the school made Camhs referrals for students when considered appropriate.

Health officials in the west of Scotland have confirmed that two injecting drug users are currently receiving hospital treatment for suspected cases of botulism. The patients, one from the Greater Glasgow and Clyde area and the other from Lanarkshire, are both in serious conditions. This brings the total number of botulism cases in Glasgow hospitals to four. The infections are believed to have been caused by contaminated heroin. In response to these new cases, NHS Forth Valley has reported treating a confirmed case in one of its hospitals as well.Dr. Catriona Milosevic, a consultant in public health medicine at NHSGGC, has issued a warning to drug users, urging them to be highly vigilant and seek urgent medical attention if they experience early symptoms such as blurred or double vision, difficulty in swallowing and speaking, and inflammation at the injection site. While it is important for injecting drug users to engage with the available services to help tackle their addictions, such as those provided by pharmacies and drug services, Dr. Milosevic emphasized that immediate medical assistance should be sought if these early symptoms manifest. She also recommended that heroin users avoid injecting the drug into their muscles, as injecting into a vein or smoking it can reduce the risk of botulism.The health board has informed Police Scotland about the new cases, and inquiries are underway to investigate further. Botulism is caused by a toxin produced by the bacterium Clostridium botulinum, which attacks the nervous system. It is a serious condition that requires prompt medical intervention. Health officials are working diligently to raise awareness about the risks associated with contaminated heroin use and to ensure that drug users are well-informed and equipped to protect themselves from this potentially life-threatening infection.

With all the places in Bloomfield you can probably do better, I was disappointed to find that the restaurant I chose didn't quite live up to my expectations. I had been looking forward to trying it out for weeks, but unfortunately, it didn't deliver.The menu had a lot of options, but the portions were small and the prices were a bit steep. I ordered the pasta dish, which was flavorful but lacked a certain je ne sais quoi that I've come to expect from Italian food.The service was also lacking - our server seemed disinterested and took a while to take our order. When she finally did, she got it wrong and brought out the wrong dish.The atmosphere was the only redeeming quality of the restaurant. It was cozy and inviting, with dim lighting and comfortable seating. But unfortunately, that wasn't enough to make up for the subpar food and service.Overall, I was underwhelmed by my experience at this restaurant and wouldn't recommend it to my friends or family. There are much better options in Bloomfield for those looking for a satisfying meal.

We prove the Kirillov-Reshetikhin (KR) conjecture in the general case : forall twisted quantum affine algebras we prove that the characters of KR modulessolve the twisted Q-system and we get explicit formulas for the character oftheir tensor products (the untwisted simply-laced case was treated by Najakima,and the untwisted case by the author). The proof is uniform and providesseveral new developments for the representation theory of twisted quantumaffine algebras, including twisted Frenkel-Reshetikhin q-characters (expectedby Frenkel-Reshetikhin and Frenkel-Mukhin). We also prove the twisted T-system.As an application we get explicit formulas for the twisted q-characters offundamental representations for all types, including the formulas for typesD_4^{(3)}, E_6^{(2)} conjectured by Reshetikhin. We prove the formulas for KRmodules in types A_n^{(2)} and D_4^{(3)} conjectured by Kuniba-Suzuki.Eventually our results imply the conjectural branching rules [HKOTT] to thequantum subalgebra of finite type.

Alright, bro, picture this: it's Obama's last year as President, and he's about to give his epic State of the Union Address, right? But then, out of nowhere, a bunch of pranksters from various subreddits decide to hack into his teleprompter! Man, talk about throwing a curveball. Obama, being the cool cat he is, notices the hilarious changes on the teleprompter and decides to roll with it like a boss. He's like, "Alright, folks, let's have some fun with this!"So, instead of delivering the usual serious and formal address, Obama starts cracking jokes left and right. He's roasting politicians, dropping some sick one-liners, and even doing impressions. The crowd is in stitches, man. They can't believe what they're witnessing.One moment, he's imitating Trump's signature voice, and the next, he's doing a spot-on impression of Joe Biden's aviators and "malarkey" catchphrase. The room is filled with laughter and applause.Obama takes a moment to reflect on his accomplishments too. He's like, "Remember when I dropped the mic at my last Correspondents' Dinner? Yeah, that was pretty epic."The pranksters continue updating the teleprompter with hilarious memes and pop culture references. Obama goes along with it, incorporating the jokes seamlessly into his speech. He talks about cats with bread on their heads, Rickrolling, and even does the Fortnite dance, much to the delight of the crowd.Throughout the speech, Obama also takes some time to address serious issues. He talks about climate change, income inequality, and the importance of unity. But he does it all with a touch of humor, making it engaging and entertaining.The whole nation is glued to their TV screens, laughing their hearts out while also pondering the serious matters that Obama addresses. It's an unprecedented mix of comedy and substance.At the end of the speech, Obama thanks the pranksters and acknowledges their creativity.

The one time I did visit, I was sorely disappointed. The atmosphere was lackluster, with generic bar decor and a noisy crowd that made it difficult to hold a conversation. As for the service, it left much to be desired. Our server seemed disinterested and inattentive, taking forever to bring us our drinks and forgetting some of our orders entirely. Moreover, the food was subpar at best. I ordered a burger, hoping for a juicy and flavorful experience, but what arrived was a dry and overcooked patty that lacked any notable seasoning. The fries were nothing to write home about either, just your average, forgettable side dish. And the prices were not justified by the quality of the food or overall experience. It felt like I was paying a premium for a below-average meal. In terms of ambiance, Bar Louie failed to impress as well. The lighting was too dim for my liking, making it difficult to even see the menu properly. The music was overly loud, drowning out any attempt at conversation. Overall, I left Bar Louie feeling unsatisfied and unimpressed. There are plenty of other dining options in town that offer better food, service, and atmosphere. I won't be returning anytime soon, if ever.

I can't believe it's been that long!), and it still remains one of my favorite spots in town. The moment I walked in, I was greeted with warm smiles and a cozy atmosphere that instantly made me feel at home. Abstract:This article examines the concept of dark energy and its impact on the field of astronomy. We argue that the idea of dark energy, a mysterious force thought to be responsible for the accelerating expansion of the universe, is fundamentally flawed and harmful to the advancement of astronomical research. We demonstrate how the assumption of dark energy's existence has led to a series of unfortunate consequences, including the neglect of alternative explanations for the observed phenomena and a lack of critical thinking in the scientific community. Furthermore, we show how the focus on dark energy has diverted resources and attention away from more pressing astrophysical questions, such as the nature of cosmic rays and the origins of the universe. We propose that a renewed focus on observation-driven research, rather than the pursuit of a preconceived idea, is necessary to advance our understanding of the cosmos. Ultimately, we suggest that the rejection of the concept of dark energy is essential for the progress of astronomy and the pursuit of scientific truth.

The sun was shining brightly on the small town of Willow Creek, casting a warm glow over the quaint streets and homes. The residents were going about their daily routines, unaware of the strange occurrence that was about to take place.In the town square, a small, unassuming shop was opening its doors for the first time. The sign above the door read "Curios and Antiques," and the window was filled with a collection of oddities and treasures.The shop's owner, an elderly man named Mr. Jenkins, was busy arranging his wares when a young girl named Lily entered the shop. She was fascinated by the strange objects on display, and Mr. Jenkins was happy to share their stories with her.As Lily wandered the aisles, she came across a small, intricately carved wooden box. She picked it up, and as she did, the box opened, revealing a glowing gemstone inside.Lily was captivated by the gemstone, and Mr. Jenkins told her it was a rare and powerful stone that had been passed down through generations of his family.Suddenly, the shop was filled with a bright light, and Lily felt herself being pulled towards the gemstone. She reached out and took it, and as she did, she felt a strange energy coursing through her body.The next thing Lily knew, she was standing in a vast, open field, surrounded by rolling hills and towering trees. She looked down and saw that she was holding the gemstone, and it was glowing brighter than ever.Lily knew that she had been transported to a magical world, and she was filled with wonder and excitement. She began to explore the field, marveling at the beauty and magic that surrounded her.As she wandered, Lily encountered a group of creatures unlike any she had ever seen. They were small and furry, with big, round ears and twitching noses. They seemed friendly, and Lily approached them cautiously.The creatures welcomed Lily warmly, and they showed her their home, a hidden burrow beneath the roots of a giant tree.

Five to break my heart:I watched as he walked away, his silhouette fading into the distance, carrying with it my hopes and dreams. The tears welled up in my eyes, blurring my vision as the ache in my chest intensified. His words echoed in my mind, each syllable slicing through my heart like a razor. I felt the weight of his indifference crushing my spirit, leaving me breathless and shattered. The memories flooded back, each one a reminder of what we once had, now lost in the depths of despair. I clutched my chest, as if trying to hold the broken pieces together, but they slipped through my fingers like grains of sand, leaving only emptiness in their wake.Five to reassemble it:But amidst the wreckage of my heart, a glimmer of hope emerged. I reached out to friends, who enveloped me in their comforting arms and soothing words. Their love and understanding slowly mended the fractures within me, piece by fragile piece. Together, we laughed, we cried, and we healed. I rediscovered the strength within myself, finding solace in the beauty of new beginnings. With each sunrise, I embraced the promise of a brighter future, leaving behind the pain of the past. In the embrace of love and self-discovery, my heart emerged, not merely repaired, but transformed – resilient and open to the endless possibilities that lay ahead.

The BBC has announced that viewers who only watch its programmes on iPlayer will soon be required to purchase a TV licence. This change in policy will come into effect on September 1st. Previously, those who only watched shows on catch-up platforms like iPlayer did not need a TV licence. However, with the increasing popularity of streaming services, the BBC has decided to update its rules to reflect the changing viewing habits of the public. The move is expected to bring in an additional £200 million in revenue for the broadcaster.

We present a detailed analysis of the Galaxy Stellar Mass Function ofgalaxies up to z=2.5 as obtained from the VVDS. We estimate the stellar massfrom broad-band photometry using 2 different assumptions on the galaxy starformation history and show that the addition of secondary bursts to acontinuous star formation history produces systematically higher (up to 40%)stellar masses. At low redshift (z=0.2) we find a substantial population oflow-mass galaxies (<10^9 Msun) composed by faint blue galaxies (M_I-M_K=0.3).In general the stellar mass function evolves slowly up to z=0.9 and moresignificantly above this redshift. Conversely, a massive tail is present up toz=2.5 and have extremely red colours (M_I-M_K=0.7-0.8). We find a decline withredshift of the overall number density of galaxies for all masses (59+-5% forM>10^8 Msun at z=1), and a mild mass-dependent average evolution(`mass-downsizing'). In particular our data are consistent with mild/negligible(<30%) evolution up to z=0.7 for massive galaxies (>6x10^10 Msun). For lessmassive systems the no-evolution scenario is excluded. A large fraction (>=50%)of massive galaxies have been already assembled and converted most of their gasinto stars at z=1, ruling out the `dry mergers' as the major mechanism of theirassembly history below z=1. This fraction decreases to 33% at z=2. Low-masssystems have decreased continuously in number and mass density (by a factor upto 4) from the present age to z=2, consistently with a prolonged mass assemblyalso at z<1.

Richard Loach performed CPR on Phil Nunnerley, 69, who had a suspected heart attack on a bus after the match against Fiji at Twickenham last Friday.Mr Nunnerley launched a social media campaign to try to track his saviour down. He only knew he was wearing a Gloucester Rugby shirt.The men have now spoken.Dr Loach, a GP on the Isle of Wight who used to live in Gloucester, got in touch with Mr Nunnerley after reading about his story in a BBC News website article.He said: "Phil's a very lucky man. It was so great to see him looking so well on that short video [in the BBC story]."I've spoken to him and he sounds so great. My wife and I were both involved and we were so chuffed to see him so well."Dr Loach said he and his wife realised Mr Nunnerley was ill while they were travelling on the bus."We were just minding our own business and we heard someone say 'stop the bus'."He didn't look very well. I had seen it before. We thought we'd better help out."Dr Loach said Mr Nunnerley, from West Harptree in North Somerset, had no pulse when they started carrying out mouth-to-mouth resuscitation and chest depressions.Four paramedics then continued to treat him when the bus got to the park and ride."Normally the outcome is not usually so good. I'm sure anyone else would have done exactly the same," said Dr Loach.Mr Nunnerley said he is planning to meet soon to say thank you in person.

The German, 31, was adjudged to have broken radio transmission rules and was demoted from second to third place.Mercedes were found to have gone beyond the degree permitted to help a driver solve a technical problem.The team said in a statement they "accept the stewards' interpretations of the regulations and their decision".Rosberg's one-place demotion meant his lead at the top of the drivers' standings was cut to a single point by team-mate Hamilton.The Formula 1 rules were changed this year to introduce restrictions on how much teams can help drivers while they are out on track.Teams are allowed to intervene if the car has a "critical problem if a failure of a component or system is imminent and potentially terminal".Race stewards found Mercedes were allowed to give Rosberg instructions on the settings he needed to resolve the problem.But they should not have responded to his subsequent question on whether he should then change gear so the car went straight through seventh gear.Rosberg's engineer Tony Ross replied to the German's information that he had a gearbox problem with the words: "Driver default 1-0-1, chassis default 0-1, chassis default 0-1. Avoid seventh gear, Nico, avoid seventh gear."Rosberg then asked: "What does that mean? I have to shift through it?"Engineer: "Affirm Nico, you need to shift through it. Affirm, you need to shift through it."

The abstract of the given title is as follows:The gravitational acceleration of the Casimir energy for a pair of perfectly conducting parallel plates is studied by considering plates undergoing acceleration perpendicular to their surfaces. The gravitational force acting on the accelerated plates is obtained from the corresponding force via the equivalence principle. The plates are treated as being in weak gravitational fields and are coupled to a massless scalar field. The force acting on each plate is found to be just its inertial mass times the gravitational acceleration. The total mass of the system consists of the bare mass of the plates renormalized by the Casimir energy of each plate, plus the energy of the Casimir interaction between the plates. This result agrees with the previously obtained result that the Casimir energy gravitates in accordance with the equivalence principle. Extension of the present work to general quantum vacuum energy configurations is in progress.

We consider the interpretation of tetrad fields as reference frames inspacetime. Reference frames may be characterized by an antisymmetricacceleration tensor, whose components are identified as the inertialaccelerations of the frame (the translational acceleration and the frequency ofrotation of the frame). This tensor is closely related togravitoelectromagnetic field quantities. We construct the set of tetrad fieldsadapted to observers that are in free fall in the Schwarzschild spacetime, andshow that the gravitational energy-momentum constructed out of this set oftetrad fields, in the framework of the teleparallel equivalent of generalrelatrivity, vanishes. This result is in agreement with the principle ofequivalence, and may be taken as a condition for a viable definition ofgravitational energy.

Melissa Priestley, 33, worked at HMP Low Newton in Durham, the prison where serial killer Rose West is held.Priestley will be sentenced next month after appearing at Durham Crown Court to admit misconduct in public office.An investigation began following a tip-off, before letters to Priestley were found in the prisoner's cell.Investigators also found messages on Priestley's mobile phone which corroborated the relationship, the CPS said.John Dilworth, of the CPS North East, said: "For people to have confidence in the Criminal Justice System, they need to know that the law applies equally to all of those involved in the delivery of justice."The relationship between Melissa Priestley and the prisoner, over whom she had a professional duty of care, was wholly inappropriate."I would like to praise the swift actions of the prison authorities and police, once the initial reports of this relationship were received."Through their diligence vital evidence was preserved, assisting greatly in the Crown's preparation of a robust case against Melissa Priestley."Priestley was bailed pending a sentencing hearing on a date yet to be set.

The zero-field and high-field phases of IPA-CuCl3 are discussed.The field-induced spin-singlet state and a field-induced order in IPA-CuCl3 are found.A magnetization plateau observed in IPA-CuCl3 is due to the field-induced phase transition from a spin liquid to a spin solid.These transitions are discussed by taking into account the effect of the staggered fields.The temperature-field phase diagram for IPA-CuCl3 is proposed.These results provide a comprehensive understanding of the exotic magnetic behaviors in IPA-CuCl3.IPA-CuCl3 is an S = 1/2 Heisenberg antiferromagnet on a triangular lattice and exhibits exotic magnetic properties.Field-induced successive phase transitions in IPA-CuCl3 are studied by the magnetization, magnetic susceptibility and specific heat measurements.

I love the music. It reflects what is just about to happen. It has saved me from more than a few relationships that would have been unsavory. I have two theories about the music. Either I am the only one who hears it, or everyone does and no one ever talks about it. Which is fine. Music is a very personal thing after all. Normally, the music is always at a comfortable level, unless it is trying to warn me of something dire. There was one moment where… One moment where the sound was deafening. There was a rising crescendo ever since I woke up that morning. I turned on the news to watch while eating breakfast, as I always do. Usually the music goes back and forth between dramatic and more more lulling, depending on the news break about to come up. But that morning it didn' t change. It just crescendoing until the newscaster spoke. "The World Trade Centers have been hit. "I dropped my fork. The ting of it hitting the table was all I could hear. For the first time, ever, my world had gone quiet. As I watched the events unfold, the music continued to be silent. Finally, after what felt like days but was only minutes, it started piping up again. A long, low clarinet played. And then other instruments slowly joined it. Something sad, but hopeful. It was playing as I was heading out the door to go to my Red Cross to see what I could do to help.

**Hillsborough Disaster: Inquest Finds Turnstile Closures Contributed to Tragedy**A jury inquest has concluded that the Hillsborough disaster, which claimed the lives of 96 football fans, could have been avoided if police had not closed 12 turnstiles. The inquest heard evidence that the turnstiles were closed to prevent ticketless fans from entering the stadium, but that this decision had led to a crush and ultimately the deaths of many innocent people. The jury found that the police had been negligent in their handling of the event and that their actions had contributed to the tragedy. The inquest's findings are a significant step towards justice for the families of the victims and survivors of the disaster.

In this study, we investigate the problem of maximal ball packings in symplectic-toric manifolds. We consider non-compact symplectic toric manifolds, which extend the classical theory of compact symplectic toric manifolds. The moment map and its associated quotient play a crucial role in our analysis. We establish that the image of the moment map in the dual of the Lie algebra of the torus determines a unimodular polytope and examine its implications for ball packings. By classifying symplectic toric manifolds in terms of degree two cohomology classes and unimodular local embeddings, we construct a framework to study the maximal density of ball packings in these manifolds. Our findings contribute to a better understanding of the geometric and topological properties of symplectic-toric manifolds, highlighting the role of ball packings in characterizing their structure.

We present broadband NIR spectra of A0620-00 obtained with SpeX on the IRTF.The spectrum is characterized by a blue continuum on which are superimposedbroad emission lines of HI and HeII and a host of narrower absorption lines ofneutral metals and molecules. Spectral type standard star spectra scaled to thedereddened spectrum of A0620-00 in K exceed the A0620-00 spectrum in J and Hfor all stars of spectral type K7V or earlier, demonstrating that the donorstar, unless later than K7V, cannot be the sole NIR flux source in A0620-00. Inaddition, the atomic absorption lines in the K3V spectrum are too weak withrespect to those of A0620-00 even at 100% donor star contribution, restrictingthe spectral type of the donor star in A0620-00 to later than K3V. Comparisonof the A0620-00 spectrum to scaled K star spectra indicates that the COabsorption features are significantly weaker in A0620-00 than in field dwarfstars. Fits of scaled model spectra of a Roche lobe-filling donor star to thespectrum of A0620-00 show that the best match to the CO absorption lines isobtained when the C abundance is reduced to [C/H] = -1.5. The donor starcontribution in the H waveband is determined to be 82+-2%. Combined withprevious published results from Froning & Robinson (2001) and Marsh et al.(1994), this gives a precise mass for the black hole in A0620-00 of M_BH =9.7+-0.6 M_solar.

" " Let' s see... Ca n' t go this way, I' ll meet up with the monster... This way? Nope, will meet with one of his baby monster eggs and it' ll eat me somehow... " I could n' t go in any direction, at least according to this little paper that I found on the floor. It says it' s a movie script for some movie. My squad did get slaughtered, so yeah, I have no better idea than follow this; it has a convenient map. " This! Yes! It says the last scene is when " Jack " dies, and there was already a dead guy there with a Jack nameta- " " Stop. " No, you stop, I thought. I could n' t get there, though; it was like the map was n' t right. It looked like I saw a light, you know, that light at the end of the tunnel crap? Yeah, I thought that; do n' t blame me, when you' re being hunted by something you do n' t think that much, and what you do think is n' t quite right, usually. " I said stop. " I was so close, but so far...! The light was getting closer, but... very slowly. I looked at the script' s map, and noticed where I was going... The map was backwards. I was heading to the place where it said I was going to die. It was too late to run back; I' m gone for good. I hear a noise behind me and release myself... I only heard a sharp stab noise, and, a bit later, a " CUT! ". " " That was a really difficult character to pull off, damn. I need a coffee. "

The Police Service of Northern Ireland (PSNI) has taken a unique approach to tackle the issue of suspects failing to appear in court. In an announcement made today, the PSNI revealed their plan to post Wild West-style pictures of these individuals on their official Facebook page. The move is aimed at encouraging community involvement in identifying and reporting these individuals.The PSNI believes that by sharing these images on social media, they can harness the power of public engagement to aid in locating and bringing these suspects to justice. The Wild West-inspired pictures will feature the suspects' faces along with a brief description of their offenses and any relevant details.This unconventional tactic has already generated a buzz among the public, with many expressing support for the initiative. However, there have been concerns raised regarding potential privacy issues and the risk of misidentification. In response, the PSNI has assured the public that all efforts will be made to ensure accuracy and protect individual rights.While the approach may seem unorthodox, the PSNI hopes it will serve as a deterrent to potential offenders who may think they can evade the justice system. By leveraging the power of social media, the police force aims to create a stronger bond with the community and foster a sense of shared responsibility in upholding the law.The PSNI's decision to embrace this new method reflects the evolving landscape of law enforcement and the increasing role of technology in crime prevention. As the initiative rolls out, it remains to be seen how effective it will be in addressing the issue of suspects failing to appear in court, but it is undoubtedly a bold step towards engaging the community in crime prevention and reducing the burden on the justice system.

He was struck on the back of the head in the seventh over and needed support from fielder George Bailey, then sat on the ground to receive treatment.Morgan played no further part, with James Taylor taking on the captaincy."He's got a lump on his head, but he's fine," said coach Trevor Bayliss."He's got a bit of a headache but he's up and about now and having a chat."When anyone gets hit it's an anxious moment, but the fact he walked off the field was a good sign. He was dizzy for a while and took an hour or so to come good. I'm sure the medical staff will keep a close eye on him."The hosts had already slipped to 22-3 in the deciding game when Morgan was replaced by Jonny Bairstow. They went on to lose by eight wickets.The blow to Morgan, who was wearing a helmet, caused immediate concern for the visiting players, less than a year after the death of Phillip Hughes, the Australia batsman who passed away after being struck on the neck by a bouncer.The fielders immediately went to help Morgan, while a shaken Starc was comforted by captain Steve Smith, coach Darren Lehmann, team-mate John Hastings and Morgan's batting partner Ben Stokes."There were a couple of guys that were a little bit shaken up, Starcy in particular," said Smith."It was a tough summer for us back home, losing a close mate. Whenever anyone gets hit like that, your first concern is for their health. Mine is that for Eoin, I hope he's OK and can recover well."The new Australia skipper added: "I don't think anyone ever means to do anything like that. It is part of the game to bowl bouncers."You have to get back on the shop as quickly as possible, you still have got a job to do. You need to take it out of your mind, but you don't like seeing anyone get hit like that."

This article presents the Corbino experimental set-up for Cooper pair mass spectroscopy and the determination of mobility in the normal phase. The Corbino set-up is a versatile platform that enables the measurement of the mass of Cooper pairs, which are pairs of electrons that form in superconductors, and their mobility in the normal phase. The experimental setup consists of a cylindrical Corbino cavity, which is designed to confine and manipulate the Cooper pairs, and a set of superconducting electrodes that are used to detect the Cooper pairs. The article describes the design and implementation of the Corbino set-up and discusses its potential for studying the properties of Cooper pairs and their role in superconductivity. The authors also demonstrate the ability to measure the mobility of Cooper pairs in the normal phase, which is an important parameter for understanding the behavior of superconductors. The Corbino set-up offers a powerful tool for investigating the properties of Cooper pairs and their role in superconductivity, and it has the potential to advance our understanding of this fundamental phenomenon.

' Anger, stop hitting the button!' Fear yelled as he and Joy tried to pull the red Emotion away from the console.' You' re going to do something really stupid! " She' s the one being silly!' Anger yelled.' Why ca n' t she see that?! " He' s sad as well as angry,' Sadness said meekly.' Ca n' t you just let someone else take over so we can sort this out and not cause a mess? " Oh, please,' Disgust sneered.' Like he' s going to make the best choice... " SHOVE OFF!' Anger yelled as he pushed the other emotions away and causing Disgust to land on a button on the console. Seconds later, the entire room was violently shaken as the lights all went out, the emotions being violently thrown around the room. When it all stopped, they were groaning as Fear was running around.' What did she do that for?' Sadness began to cry.' Way. To. Go,' Disgust said coldly as she stormed past Anger.' You' ve ruined the poor boy' s life. " What makes you say that?' Anger furiously demanded. It was then that Disgust held him around the neck and lifted him upward so he was directly in her face.' Because the button you forced me to press was the Impulse Choice button,' Disgust sneered.' The button which forces an appropriate action based on which Emotion presses it, and I do n' t think that Lily will ever forgive Severus for calling her a Mudblood, will she?'

We lived in the projects. My dad had just joined the military and my mom worked late afternoons. I had come home from school and danced in the dining room, moving around the tables. No one else was home. No one else was ever home. I was by myself often, my sister at friends and my parents working. I remember the floor creaking with every step I took. The sky had looked grey and I wanted to go outside to play. I was 10 at the time. We had just played yesterday. And I remember him sitting me down and we were practice kissing. We were n' t in love, we were n' t going to grow up together. We were n' t anything but two kids just kissing because it felt good. Because that is what happens you are 10 and bored and left alone. I remember someone had told on us and I was grounded and then we moved to another base like a month later. I do n' t know what happened to him, nor frankly do I feel like I had some requited lost love. It was just fun. It felt good. That' s all that matters when you are a kid.

It also cut its profit forecast for 2017 by 4.3%.Eurotunnel assumes the pound will be 7% lower versus the euro, reducing its profits when converted back into euros.Two thirds of its income is from vehicle shuttle services, and UK customers make up 80% of its car passengers.Shares in Eurotunnel, which runs train services between Britain and France, fell 30% after the EU referendum vote and are still 16% below pre-Brexit levels.The firm said the impact of Brexit on the level of cross-Channel transport was uncertain, but that it did not expect any significant impact on its activities in the short term.Chief Executive Jacques Gounon said: "Despite the financial market uncertainty generated by the United Kingdom voting to leave the European Union, the Group remains confident in the performance of its economic model and in its outlook."However, other factors have had an impact on its business. Eurostar's passenger traffic fell 3% in the first half of 2016 due to attacks in Brussels in March and rail strikes in Belgium and France.Meanwhile truck shuttle traffic was up 10% in the same period. The food not at all. First time here, wife and I sat at the bar and people watched over some WELL POURED drinks. Strong point. Then we screwed up and ordered food. I got their infamous lobster roll. It is proposed that radiation belts similar to the ones in the planetarymagnetosphere can exist for a pulsar with a relatively long period and a strongmagnetic field. In the belts located in the closed field line region near thelight cylinder relativistic pairs are trapped and maintained at a densitysubstantially higher than the local Goldreich-Julian corotation density. Thetrapped plasma can be supplied and replenished by either direct injection ofrelativistic pairs from acceleration of externally-supplied particles in adormant outer gap or in situ ionization of the accreted neutral material in thetrapping region. The radiation belts can be disrupted by waves that are excitedin the region as the result of plasma instabilities or emitted from the surfacedue to starquakes or stellar oscillations. The disruption can cause anintermittent particle precipitation toward the star producing radio bursts. Itis suggested that such bursts may be seen as rotating radio transients (RRATs).

Sure, here's a continuation of the review in a human style:Living in the burbs and spending most of my time in the city, I always look and seek out some natural green space now and then. It's easy to feel suffocated by the concrete jungle, and sometimes you just need a breath of fresh air. That's why I was thrilled to stumble upon a hidden gem in the heart of the city - a tranquil park that'shome to a diverse array of plant and animal life. Sandcastle Water Park in P-Burgh was just "okay" for me. I had pretty high expectations going in, but it didn't quite live up to them. The park itself is massive, no doubt about that. Water slides and pools everywhere you look! But the first thing that struck me was the long lines. I mean, seriously, I've never seen queues this long in my life. It felt like we spent more time waiting than actually enjoying the rides. Not cool, Sandcastle, not cool at all.And speaking of the rides, they were a bit underwhelming. I tried a few of the big ones, like the Thunder Run and the Blue Tubular Twister, but they didn't really get my adrenaline pumping. I felt like they were missing that thrill factor, you know? Maybe I've been to too many water parks, but these rides just didn't live up to my expectations.On the positive side, they had a decent lazy river. It was nice to kick back and relax while floating along the gentle current. The wave pool was also fun, although it got way too crowded later in the day. People were practically on top of each other, and let me tell you, that's not my idea of a good time. Plus, finding a free lounge chair was like finding a needle in a haystack. Every spot seemed to be taken, and it ended up being a real struggle to find a place to chill out and soak up some sun.The food options were lackluster as well. I was hoping for some tasty snacks or a decent meal, but all they had were overpriced hot dogs and soggy fries. I mean, come on, Sandcastle, you can do better than that! And don't even get me started on the prices. Everything was way too expensive, from the admission tickets to the lockers and even the parking. It felt like they were just trying to squeeze every penny out of us.To be fair, there were some highlights. The staff was friendly and helpful, despite the chaos around them. They did their best to keep things running smoothly and ensure everyone was having a good time.

Partick Thistle manager Alan Archibald is confident that the Firhill pitch will be ready to stage its first Scottish Premiership match for three weeks. The pitch has been undergoing renovation work, but Archibald believes it will be ready for the visit of Celtic on Friday."The pitch is looking good," Archibald said. "The guys have been working hard on it, and I'm confident it will be ready for Friday."The Firhill pitch has been a source of concern for Thistle this season, with several matches being postponed due to its poor condition. However, Archibald is optimistic that the work being done will improve the situation."We've had a few issues with the pitch this season, but I think we're turning a corner now," he said. "The players are looking forward to playing on a better surface, and I'm sure it will make a big difference."Despite the pitch concerns, Thistle have been in good form of late, winning their last two matches against St Johnstone and Kilmarnock. Archibald is hoping that his team can continue their momentum against Celtic."We're going into the game with confidence," he said. "We know it's going to be a tough match, but we're ready for the challenge."Celtic, who are currently top of the Scottish Premiership, will provide a stern test for Thistle. However, Archibald believes that his team can cause an upset."We've been working on our tactics, and we think we have a plan that can hurt Celtic," he said. "We know we'll have to be at our best, but we're ready for it."The match against Celtic is the start of a difficult run of fixtures for Thistle, who also face Rangers and Aberdeen in the coming weeks. Archibald is aware of the challenges ahead, but he is focused on the task at hand."We're taking it one game at a time," he said. "We know we have some tough matches coming up, but we're just concentrating on Celtic for now."The match against Celtic is expected to be a close encounter, with both teams in good form.

A normal day. That' s all it was - and it became routine- responding to Jumpers. That' s what we called them - despite the fact that we always able talked them down, the ones we caught. The fog rolled in, it was thick and you could probably only see 10 feet ahead of you. It was quite eerie. You know, it took a toll on you to see people so distressed, as if life was n' t worth living. My life is the force. I do n' t have any kids, nor am I married. It' s strange, because I do get lonely, and I do long for a special someone. I just do n' t have time. Time it' s a strange thing- my shift crawls- The only time I feel alive is when I' m helping those in need. It was October 12th 2013, 12: 15 p. m, the thick fog blocked the sun and it was completely grey. It was by sheer chance that I saw him on the red rails of the bridge as I was patrolling through, I stopped abruptly, threw my lights on and I approached the man. White male, age roughly 30 years old. I approached him, and called, " Sir, I' m here to help! " He was a few feet away. He was dressed in black rags, his head scraped, and smelled like death. It was entirely unusual to see someone in this physical condition as bad as him. Rotting flesh- that' s the smell. I called out to him as traffic zoomed by, just missing my squad car, " Sir, my name is Officer Heart! " He replied, " I know. " His head still peering into the white abyss below. He turned to me, we locked eyes, and he said, " I know who you are Heart. " I was looking into my blue eyes, I observed the scar on his face - it was in the precise location where I got mine. His eyes were far more piercing than my own - they peered deeply into my soul. He said, " I' ve been waiting for you. " My hands felt ice cold - my hat flew off- the wind rushed through my hair - and i felt weightless. I struggled to breathe. Than I realized I was falling and right before impact - I realized - I was the jumper.

In this study, we investigate the challenges encountered in the search for the Higgs boson within the framework of the Minimal Supersymmetric Standard Model (MSSM) at hadron colliders. The MSSM, an extension of the Standard Model, predicts the existence of multiple Higgs bosons with distinct properties. We analyze the impact of various factors, including large tan beta values, supersymmetry breaking scenarios, and the scale at which supersymmetry breaking is communicated to the visible sector, on the search for MSSM Higgs bosons. We find that the rare decays involving B-mesons, such as b -> s gamma and B_s -> mu+ mu-, can provide valuable constraints on the MSSM parameter space, particularly for high scale supersymmetry breaking. Additionally, we consider the implications of direct searches for the Higgs boson at the Tevatron and the prospects of future searches at the Large Hadron Collider (LHC). Furthermore, we explore the interplay between MSSM Higgs searches and direct dark matter detection experiments, shedding light on the complementarity of these approaches. Our comprehensive analysis underscores the importance of considering multiple experimental constraints and highlights the potential of the LHC and future experiments in probing MSSM Higgs scenarios with low mass and large tan beta.

As news of Albert's passing spread, the villagers gathered around his graveside, their hearts heavy with grief. Abstract:In this study, we investigate the behavior of a one-dimensional quantum dot hosting a deformed Wigner crystal. By employing a variety of theoretical and computational techniques, we unveil the intricate properties and emergent phenomena arising in this system. We begin by analyzing the formation and stability of the deformed Wigner crystal under different confinement potentials and electron densities. Our results reveal that the deformed Wigner crystal configuration can be achieved by appropriately tuning the external parameters, leading to a rich variety of structural deformations. Furthermore, we delve into the dynamics of the deformed Wigner crystal, exploring its response to external perturbations and thermal effects. Through extensive simulations, we elucidate the influence of various factors such as temperature, electron-electron interactions, and external fields on the behavior of the crystal. Our findings demonstrate that the deformed Wigner crystal exhibits distinctive dynamic properties, including collective oscillations and rearrangements of the electron density distribution. Finally, we investigate the impact of disorder and impurities on the deformed Wigner crystal, highlighting the effects of disorder-induced localization and the emergence of localized states within the crystal. Overall, this work provides a comprehensive understanding of the deformed Wigner crystal in the context of one-dimensional quantum dots, opening new avenues for the exploration of novel quantum phenomena in low-dimensional systems.

As I took my final breath, the world around me faded into darkness. But instead of the expected void, I found myself standing in a radiant realm of pure light. Confusion washed over me, and as my vision adjusted, I saw a figure approaching. It was a dog, wagging its tail with boundless joy. As it drew nearer, a sense of familiarity washed over me. It was my childhood companion, Max, who had passed away years ago. I couldn't believe my eyes.Tears filled my eyes as I knelt down and embraced him, feeling his presence, his warmth. But as I looked into his eyes, I saw something extraordinary. It was a depth of wisdom and love that transcended anything I had ever known. It was then that I realized this was no ordinary dog. Standing before me was God, taking the form of my beloved Max.In that moment, I felt an overwhelming surge of gratitude and awe. I had always imagined meeting God in human form, but this incarnation was even more beautiful. Max, or rather, God, guided me through the heavenly realm, showing me the wonders and mysteries that lay beyond mortal comprehension.In this divine realm, there were no boundaries, no pain, no suffering. Only pure love and joy permeated every inch of existence. God, as Max, explained that all dogs go to heaven because they embody the purest essence of unconditional love.As we explored this ethereal paradise together, I felt a sense of oneness with every living being. I understood the interconnectedness of all souls, both human and animal. Conversations with God were no longer whispered prayers but heartfelt barks filled with understanding.In this heavenly realm, I discovered my purpose—to spread love and kindness, just as dogs do on Earth. With God's guidance, I played a role in guiding lost souls towards the light, offering comfort and compassion to those who needed it most.Time ceased to hold any meaning in this divine realm, but I knew that my journey was far from over.

We report the discovery of a new white dwarf/M dwarf binary, HS1857+5144,identified in the Hamburg Quasar Survey (HQS). Time-resolved opticalspectroscopy and photometry were carried out to determine the properties ofthis new cataclysmic variable progenitor pre-CV). The light curves ofHS1857+5144 display a sinusoidal variation with a period of Porb=383.52 min andpeak-to-peak amplitudes of 0.7 mag and 1.1 mag in the B-band and R-band,respectively. The large amplitude of the brightness variation results from areflection effect on the heated inner hemisphere of the companion star,suggesting a very high temperature of the white dwarf. Our radial velocitystudy confirms the photometric period as the orbital period of the system. Amodel atmosphere fit to the spectrum of the white dwarf obtained at minimumlight provides limits to its mass and temperature of Mwd=~0.6-1.0 Msun andTwd=~70000-100000 K, respectively. The detection of HeII 4686 absorptionclassifies the primary star of HS1857+5144 as a DAO white dwarf. Combining theresults from our spectroscopy and photometry, we estimate the mass of thecompanion star and the binary inclination to be Msec=~0.15-0.30 Msun andi=~45-55 deg, respectively. We classify HS1857+5144 as one of the youngestpre-CV known to date. The cooling age of the white dwarf suggests that thepresent system has just emerged from a common envelope phase ~10^5 yr ago.HS1857+5144 will start mass transfer within or below the 2-3h period gap.

The environment of this "cantina" is distinctive; tucked in the corner of a peruvian arts store, and co-owned by the people who own Pamela's, this place has "hit" once, and "missed" once. They do have a very basic menu which doesn't change, but the items on there are those that you can pretty easily make at home. The daily specials that they have are the ones that are a bit more elaborate, and come with a few sides, such as rice and french bread. nnI previously thought that peruvian food was simply chicken and rice. Although this is still the main two ingredients they use, this restaurant has definitely changed my perspective on how many different ways they can prepare chicken. The first time I went I got a "ropa vieja", which was a very simple meat-only sandwich. The second time I went I got a chicken dish that was mixed with dried fruits: that was delicious. However my fried ordered a grounded pork dish that ended up tasting like glorified taco meat. Unfortunately that didn't make for a great first impression. nnOverall, be wary of what peruvian food specializes in, and order accordingly. Service is very friendly, and food comes out in 5 minutes because everything is bascially premade at the beginning of the day.

Cold dark matter haloes are populated by caustics, which are yet to beresolved in N-body simulations or observed in the Universe. Secondary infallmodel provides a paradigm for the study of caustics in "typical" haloesassuming that they have had no major mergers and have grown only by smoothaccretion. This is a particular characteristic of the smallest dark matterhaloes of about 10^{-5} Mo, which although "atypical" contain no substructuresand could have survived until now with no major mergers. Thus using this modelas the first guidline, we evaluate the neutralino self-annihilation flux forthese haloes. Our results show that caustics could leave a distinct sawteethsignature on the differential and cumulative fluxes coming from the outerregions of these haloes. The total annihilation signal from the regions awayfrom the centre can be boosted by about forty percents.

**Abstract:**This paper presents a three-dimensional (3D) simulation method for electromagnetic fields in nanostructures using the time-harmonic finite-element method (FEM). The proposed method is based on a novel formulation of the time-harmonic Maxwell's equations that allows for the efficient and accurate solution of large-scale 3D problems. The method is validated by comparing its results with those obtained from analytical solutions and measurements. The method is then used to investigate the electromagnetic properties of a variety of nanostructures, including plasmonic waveguides, metamaterials, and photonic crystals. The results of these simulations provide new insights into the behavior of electromagnetic fields in nanostructures and can be used to design and optimize nanostructured devices.

We propose a new mechanism to produce ultracold polar molecules withmicrowave fields. The proposed mechanism converts trapped ultracold atoms ofdifferent species into vibrationally excited molecules by a single microwavetransition and entirely depends on the existence of a permanent dipole momentin the molecules. As opposed to production of molecules by photoassociation ormagnetic-field Feshbach resonances our method does not rely on the structureand lifetime of excited states or existence of Feshbach resonances. Inaddition, we determine conditions for optimal creation of polar molecules invibrationally excited states of the ground-state potential by changingfrequency and intensity of the microwave field. We also explore the possibilityto produce vibrationally cold molecules by combining the microwave field withan optical Raman transition or by applying a microwave field to Feshbachmolecules. The production mechanism is illustrated for two polar molecules: KRband RbCs.

But as days turned into weeks, Daniel started to feel a twinge of homesickness. Despite the wonders he encountered in this parallel dimension, he longed for the familiarity of his own world. With a heavy heart, he made the decision to return. Standing before a mirror that would lead him back, he took one last look at the mirrored reality before stepping through.
