Dear [Ex],I'm not sure where to start. I've been thinking about writing this letter for weeks, but every time I sit down to do it, I end up crumpling up the paper and throwing it away. It's like I'm trying to put my thoughts and feelings into words, but they just don't want to cooperate.I guess I'll start with the basics. I miss you. I miss the way you make me laugh, the way you always know how to make me feel better when I'm down, and the way you always challenge me to be my best self.But I also hate you. I hate the way you could be so cruel, the way you would try to control me, and the way you never seemed to understand me.I know that we were together for a long time, and that we shared so many amazing memories. But I can't help but feel that it was all a lie. That you were never really the person I thought you were.I remember the first time we met, like it was yesterday. You were so charming, so confident, and so different from anyone I had ever met before. I was drawn to you like a moth to a flame.But over time, things changed. You started to show your true colors, and I realized that I had fallen for a person who didn't really exist.I tried to ignore it, to convince myself that it didn't matter. But it did matter. It mattered a lot.I know that I'm not perfect either. I've made mistakes, and I've hurt you just as much as you've hurt me. But the difference is, I'm willing to admit my mistakes and try to make amends. You never were.I'm not sure what the future holds, but I know that it's not you. It's time for us to move on, and find happiness with other people.I hope that one day we can be friends, or at least civil. But for now, I need to let you go.Goodbye, [Ex].

The 37-year-old striker from Esher has already pleaded guilty to possessing cocaine at Guildford Crown Court.He faced charges after an incident in which he was reportedly Tasered by police responding to reports of a break-in at his home in Claremont Lane.Judge Stephen Climie told him a custodial sentence would be justified.Adjourning sentencing until 12 February, the judge told Bent: "I expect the Probation Service to prepare a report on you which you are required to attend for interview to discuss in greater detail the background to what on paper is a disturbing set of events, which I'm sure you appreciate."On the face of it a custodial sentence is justified."Whether that is the right sentence in the context of this case is not a matter I will prejudge."Bent had denied two other charges of possessing bladed articles, namely a meat cleaver and a kitchen knife, which will be left on file.He had previously denied affray but changed his plea on Wednesday.The ex-England Under-21 international played 574 games and scored 113 goals during his professional career.He played for 14 different clubs including Wigan Athletic, Charlton Athletic, Everton and Leicester City.Bent also played as a striker for Sheffield United, Blackburn Rovers, Ipswich Town and Birmingham City.He was released on unconditional bail.

I love Girasole for lunch because it offers a variety of delicious and fresh Italian dishes at reasonable prices. The restaurant is small and cozy, with a homey atmosphere that makes you feel like you're dining in someone's kitchen. The decor is simple but charming, with red and white checkered tablecloths and a few Italian posters on the walls. The service is always friendly and attentive, with a warm and welcoming smile from the staff.The menu offers a range of options, from classic Italian dishes like spaghetti carbonara and lasagna, to more modern creations like pumpkin ravioli and eggplant parmesan. The ingredients are always fresh and of high quality, with a focus on locally sourced produce and meats. The portions are generous, so you'll never leave feeling hungry.I've tried several dishes at Girasole, and every single one has been delicious. The pasta is cooked to perfection, with a nice balance of flavors and textures. The sauces are rich and full-bodied, with a depth of flavor that is hard to find elsewhere. The pizza is also excellent, with a crispy crust and a flavorful sauce that is made fresh daily.The best part about Girasole is the price. For the quality of food and service you receive, the prices are incredibly reasonable. You can get a filling and delicious lunch for under $15, which is a great value in my opinion.Overall, I highly recommend Girasole for a quick and delicious lunch in a cozy and welcoming atmosphere. It's a hidden gem that is worth seeking out, and I'm sure you won't be disappointed.

Using an effective Lagrangian approach, the production of the scalar $D_{sJ}(2317)$ meson in relativistic heavy-ion collisions is studied within a transport model. The hadronic interactions of the $D_{sJ}(2317)$ meson are implemented, and the $D_{sJ}(2317)$ meson production cross sections in elementary nucleon-nucleon collisions are calculated and compared with existing experimental data. The calculated yield and transverse momentum spectra of the $D_{sJ}(2317)$ meson in Au+Au collisions at $\sqrt{s_{NN}}$ = 200 GeV are presented. The effects of the hadronic interactions and the $D_{sJ}(2317)$ meson width on its production are discussed. It is found that the hadronic interactions can significantly affect the $D_{sJ}(2317)$ meson production, especially at low transverse momentum. The calculated results for the $D_{sJ}(2317)$ meson yield and $p_{T}$ spectra can be tested in future experiments at the Relativistic Heavy Ion Collider (RHIC) and the Large Hadron Collider (LHC).

**35 Russian Diplomats Expelled by Obama Leave U.S.**WASHINGTON — The 35 Russian diplomats expelled from the United States by President Barack Obama have left the country, Russian news agencies reported Thursday.The diplomats were given 72 hours to leave the United States after the Obama administration said they had engaged in espionage and other activities harmful to U.S. national security.The expulsions were the largest since the Cold War, and they further strained relations between the United States and Russia.Russia has denied the allegations of espionage, and President Vladimir Putin has said that the expulsions were unjustified.The expulsions are a significant blow to Russia's diplomatic presence in the United States.The diplomats left the United States on commercial flights from Washington, D.C., and New York City.They were accompanied by their families.

His story will undoubtedly inspire others to find strength and determination even in the face of unimaginable adversity.

The walk up the driveway is as long and boring as usual. *This is why people have short driveways, * I think to myself, as I finally jog up the steps to my front door. After fumbling with the locks for few moments I enter inside, and smell the familiar scents of home. It always reminds me of Lucy, and it always makes me wonder when she' s coming back. *Is she coming back? * I shake the thought away as best I can and throw my coat up on a hook before I head down the hallway, past the kitchen and into my study. I' m practically *dreaming* of the butter biscuits I have saved from yesterday, but they' ll have to wait. If I do n' t get this report filed, I' m in deep trouble. The air is stale in my study, which is n' t a great thing. I' ve been falling steadily behind ever since Lucy left, and I have n' t been committing as many hours to work as I should be. Then again, maybe that' s why she left me. *John* probably does n' t have a full time job. I bet *John* just vacations 5 days a week on Lucy' s dime. The money I won her in the lawsuit is definitely enough to cover that for a few years. *Could you stop thinking about her for one goddamned second? * I shake my head again, and decide the butter biscuits are becoming more of a necessity for me than anything else right now. So I drop my work bag off at the desk and walk around the corner into my kitchen, where I stop short as all thoughts of butter biscuits fly from my mind. There' s a white grand piano. In my kitchen. There was n' t one before. But now there' s a white grand piano in my kitchen nook, right where my table used to be. " What the fuck, " I say aloud.

**Abstract**This research and development (R&D) article presents a comprehensive exploration of future Zeplin, a groundbreaking airship concept designed to revolutionize long-distance transportation. The proposed Zeplin incorporates innovative technologies and materials to achieve unprecedented efficiency, sustainability, and safety. The key features of the future Zeplin include a lightweight and aerodynamic design, advanced propulsion systems, and a state-of-the-art navigation and control system. This article discusses the technical challenges and potential solutions associated with the development of the future Zeplin, including the optimization of its design, the integration of novel materials, and the implementation of advanced control algorithms. Furthermore, it explores the broader implications of the future Zeplin, such as its potential impact on global trade, environmental sustainability, and the future of transportation. Overall, this R&D article provides a compelling vision for the future of Zeplin technology and highlights the significant potential benefits it offers for society.

In a bid to address a significant budget shortfall, Manchester City Council has announced its intention to implement cost-cutting measures that will result in the loss of 160 jobs. The council is seeking to save up to £75 million over the course of the next three years. The decision comes as a response to mounting financial pressures faced by the local authority, necessitating tough choices to ensure its long-term sustainability.The proposed job cuts have raised concerns among council employees and the wider community. The affected positions span various departments and functions, including administrative roles and frontline services. While the council has stated its commitment to minimizing the impact on essential services, critics worry that the reductions may result in a decline in overall service quality and accessibility.Council officials have emphasized the need for swift action, citing the urgency of addressing the budget shortfall. They argue that these measures are essential to maintain a balanced fiscal position and protect the council's ability to deliver core services to residents. However, trade unions and worker representatives are urging the council to explore alternative avenues to reduce costs, such as reviewing non-essential expenditures and seeking additional funding sources.The affected employees will be offered support and guidance through this transitional period, including assistance in finding alternative employment opportunities. Manchester City Council will engage in a consultation process to gather feedback and consider alternative proposals before finalizing the job cuts. The council aims to strike a balance between fiscal responsibility and safeguarding vital services while striving to mitigate the impact on both staff and the community.These substantial adjustments reflect the broader financial challenges faced by local authorities across the country.

We verify that the dilaton together with one exactly marginal field, form amoduli space of marginal deformations of closed bosonic string field theory topolynomial order five. We use the results of this successful check in order tofind the best functional form of a fit of quintic amplitudes. We then use thisfit in order to accurately compute the tachyon and dilaton effective potentialin the limit of infinite level. We observe that to order four, the effectivepotential gives unexpectedly accurate results for the vacuum. We are thus ledto conjecture that the effective potential, to a given order, is a goodapproximation to the whole potential including all interactions from thevertices up to this order from the untruncated string field. We then go on andcompute the effective potential to order five. We analyze its vacuum structureand find that it has several saddle points, including the Yang-Zwiebach vacuum,but also a local minimum. We discuss the possible physical meanings of thesevacua.

Nearly 100 businesses have pledged their support for a bid aiming to secure £1 billion in funding for the development of Edinburgh and the wider south-east region of Scotland. The project, which has received positive endorsement from both the private sector and the Scottish government, aims to drive economic growth and create new investment opportunities in the area. Previous investments in the region have laid a strong foundation for this ambitious initiative, and the support from these businesses is a testament to their confidence in the potential of the project. The bid also highlights a collaborative approach, with various entities from both the public and private sectors coming together to pursue this shared vision of bolstering the region's economy. Public consultations are already underway to gather input and ensure that the project aligns with the community's needs and aspirations as well.

It's got a busy, friendly vibe and overall satisfying food. nnThe good: to contradict a previous reviewer, I found the beer selection to be quite respectable. You've got mainstream goat piss, mainstream Irish, beer snob local, national and international. I was very pleased by the Gaelic ale from Asheville. Very pleased. Also, the nachos were truly a work of art. I can't stand flour tortillas but flour tortilla chips are great for this mouthwatering mountain of peppers, beans, tomatoes, olives, cream and little chicken bits. nnThe ok: well, I found my pasta - the Honey Chicken - to be just ok. The chicken itself was nice and juicy. I don't know what I expected from the pasta. I think it was overall a bit sweet, and perhaps a nice pungent feta topping would have broken that up a bit better. What it came with was a bland kind of Parmesan, I think. nnI might return and try something else on the menu - like their massive 1/2 pound burgers - and we'll see if it'll make me a fangirl.

The Improving Young People's Mental Health Services forum was set up by the relatives of 15-year-old Oliver Edwards-Cavill, who took his own life in 2008.Both Pembrokeshire council and Hywel Dda Health Board were due to be on the panel, but both were unable to attend.Hywel Dda said it was down to staff sickness.The headmistress of Greenhill  School and representatives from Pembrokeshire College, Age Cymru and the mental health charity Hafal were among those to attend the event in Pembroke Dock.Some audience members were frustrated by the council and health board's absence from the panel.Libby Ryan-Davies, director of Mental Health and Learning Disabilities for Hywel Dda University Health Board, said it was down to staff sickness and despite attempts, they were unable find a replacement.She said a staff representative participated as an audience member.Pembrokeshire council has been asked to comment.Some of the issues discussed were the safeguarding of children in schools and a reduction of local authority services around mental health.Oliver's aunt Suzannah Palmer, hoped the forum would stop another family "having to bury their child".Oliver's cousin, London barrister Matthew Watkins, told BBC Wales that since his death, they have read about other young people taking their lives."Over the past few years, there have been a number of children who have taken their lives and from what we have seen there has not been a huge amount of support, he said."We want to go about it the right way."We want to engage with all the services because we are not mental health specialists; we need to be informed where we can direct our efforts."Pembrokeshire council said there are "a range of universal school-based support available to secondary school pupils in Pembrokeshire, as well as targeted support provided in school by outside agencies".There is also "specific support for complex young people", the council added.

Verdict? It was so-so. We had reservations at 7: 15 and were seated promptly, which was fine, except we were seated OUTSIDE! Listen folks, it's COLD when the sun goes down. They have three tables set outside, I guess trying to squeeze out some more business. Our server was nice, but not attentive at all. We were left waiting for things for 10-20 minutes at a time. Also, because we were sitting on the sidewalk, people and animals and cars kept sweeping by us. It took the waiter three tries to get our meal order because he had to keep stepping out of the way of people walking down the street. Wine was good. Food was ehhhhhh. We had the mussel and clam appetizer and when it came out, about 1/2 of the order was not edible and all the innards had fallen into the sauce, which by the way smelled like stinky cheese. My dinner and my husbands dinner was good, no real complaints there. Espresso and a S'more cupcake for dessert and I grand total of $134 later before tip? Definitely not worth it at all. The best part of the night? Leaving. The worst part of the night? Associating this place with my anniversary.

Italian police have successfully apprehended mafia boss Roberto Pannunzi following his deportation from Colombia. Pannunzi, known as the "Cocaine King," is a prominent figure in the international drug trade. Authorities believe he had been coordinating large-scale cocaine smuggling operations between South America and Europe for several years. His arrest marks a significant victory for law enforcement agencies combating transatlantic drug rings. Pannunzi's capture was made possible through a joint effort between Italian agents, the US Drug Enforcement Administration, and Colombian authorities. The arrest is expected to deliver a severe blow to the criminal networks involved in drug trafficking, as Pannunzi's influence and expertise were instrumental in their operations.

The room was silent except for the occasional fall of incense ash. Its scent filled the room and I inhaled deeply. Then slowly let it out. Inhale deeply then slowly let it out. Man, what was the first thing I was going to do with magic? Flying? I' ve heard flying is quite fun. I did n' t have the money for a jet pack, but with magic I could fly on my own accord. It would be so amazing to touch the clouds and see the city below me. Or perhaps invisibility or clairovoyance? Thoughts of sneaking or seeing into the girls' locker room filled my mind. Visions of tits, pussies of and ass filled my head and my pants became tighter. Especially that one cheerleader. Focus! Meditation takes complete focus. Breathe in and out. In and out. I wonder how I did on my calculus test last week? I feel like I did good. But maybe not. Focus! Magic! Pew pew. Laser beams. I could create my own jet pack or even my own flying car! Ah, fuck it. That' s enough meditation for one day. It would take about five years after I figure out how to meditate for hours. As I checked the clock, I realized it had only been ten minutes. Well, you got ta start somewhere.

Parton showers are widely used to generate fully exclusive final statesneeded to compare theoretical models to experimental observations. While, ingeneral, parton showers give a good description of the experimental data, theprecise functional form of the probability distribution underlying the eventgeneration is generally not known. The reason is that realistic parton showersare required to conserve four-momentum at each vertex. In this paper weinvestigate in detail how four-momentum conservation is enforced in a standardparton shower and why this destroys the analytic control of the probabilitydistribution. We show how to modify a parton shower algorithm such that itconserves four-momentum at each vertex, but for which the full analytic form ofthe probability distribution is known. We then comment how this analyticcontrol can be used to match matrix element calculations with parton showers,and to estimate effects of power corrections and other uncertainties in partonshowers.

Curious. That is the nature of humanity. People are curious, and I am curious about them. How can they lay motionless for so many consecutive hours? There is so much to do in this life, but everyone chooses to pretend as if they' re dead for eight hours a night, or day, or whenever, honestly. The time is irrelevant; sleep is inefficient. It is lazy. *But what about dreams? * they ask me. *Dreams are where you can live out a life you would n' t otherwise be able to live. Dreams are fascinating, terrifying, and inspiring. Dreams are where our imaginations are not constrained by the confines of reality -- they are where space and time are nonsensical, and you can have a banana for an uncle, but it would still make sense, because it' s a dream! * I admit, this dreaming thing is the most curious of all things to me. Reality is all that I know. I have walked from shore to shore, and swam from beach to beach. In the night time, I have raced the yellows in my Maserati, which I was able to purchase after my insight into stock and option trading -- something I professed over the course of a few nights. I have written sonnets to the sunset, and odes to the sunrise. I have been told that I am a true virtuoso at many musical crafts, but it is only because I do not waste time " sleeping. " But dream... That is something I' ve not done. To a man who has done everything, there is little mystery that I experience anymore. If sleep is the cousin of death, then is suicide the permanent form of a sleeping pill? I do wish to dream. I do wish to dream...

In the heart of a sprawling metropolis, amidst towering skyscrapers and bustling crowds, an enigmatic experiment unfolded. Unbeknownst to the masses, a clandestine prison known as "The Oasis" lay hidden in plain sight. Its exterior bore the guise of a tranquil park, lush with foliage and blooming flowers, but behind its serene facade, a sinister secret was concealed.Within the labyrinthine depths of The Oasis, prisoners languished in a state of blissful ignorance. Their captors had meticulously crafted a perfect illusion, veiling their confinement in layers of deception. Each prisoner was assigned a unique identity and purpose, and they were led to believe that their roles within the park contributed to a greater good.Unbeknownst to them, the park's idyllic beauty was nothing more than an elaborate facade. The trees swayed not in the rhythm of nature but were programmed to mimic its motions. The azure sky was a projection, and the birdsong that echoed through the air was carefully curated to create an illusion of freedom.A young woman named Lily found herself entangled in this intricate web. She had been falsely accused of a crime she didn't commit, her entire existence reduced to a meticulously crafted lie. Assigned the role of a gardener, Lily tended to the park's flora, blissfully unaware of the prison that surrounded her.Lily's curiosity began to stir as she noticed subtle inconsistencies in her surroundings. The conversations of her fellow prisoners seemed scripted, and their laughter lacked genuine warmth. She discovered hidden cameras and microphones, carefully concealed within the park's infrastructure, monitoring their every move.With each revelation, Lily's determination to uncover the truth grew stronger. She embarked on a perilous journey, venturing into the hidden corners of The Oasis, unraveling its secrets one thread at a time.

The two bodies were found on the coastal path in Petitor Woods in Torquay.David Cauldwell, 39, was reported missing from the town on 22 May.A spokeswoman for Devon and Cornwall Police said there was no link between the two bodies found at the site.Click here for live updates on this storyOfficers first found the human remains - believed to have been there for a "considerable" time - at 20:45 BST on Tuesday. They discovered a second body, believed to be that of Mr Cauldwell, at about 08:10 BST on Wednesday.Police said: "David Cauldwell's family have been informed of the developments but formal identification has not yet taken place."The area has been cordoned off and a full investigation is being carried out to establish the circumstances of both deaths. Support will be offered to families and friends when the identities have been confirmed."It is understood police believe they know the identity of the human remains due to an item discovered where they were found.Mr Cauldwell was last seen leaving the Artful Dodger pub in Teignmouth Road, Torquay, at about midnight on 21 May.

In a small coffee shop tucked away on a quiet street, two individuals sat at separate tables, absorbed in their own worlds. The young woman, Maggie, wore a melancholic expression as she sipped her latte, while the middle-aged man, David, stared absentmindedly into his untouched cup of tea. Unbeknownst to them, destiny had intertwined their paths for a momentous encounter. As fate would have it, their eyes met, locking in a glance that stirred something deep within their souls. Time seemed to stand still as a profound connection ignited between them. In that brief moment, they saw glimpses of each other's joys, sorrows, and unspoken dreams. A shared understanding passed between them, transcending words. Maggie felt a surge of warmth and hope envelope her, lifting the weight of her burdens. David, too, found solace in Maggie's gaze, a flicker of encouragement in a world that had grown cold. Emboldened by this exchange, they both mustered the courage to approach one another. With timid steps, they closed the physical distance that had separated them moments ago. Their eyes remained locked, their gazes never wavering as they drew nearer. As they stood face to face, their unspoken connection grew stronger, enveloping them in a cocoon of empathy and understanding. No words were needed as they communicated with a language that surpassed the limitations of speech. Their eye contact became a bridge that transcended their individual experiences, merging their hearts and souls. From that day forward, Maggie and David became inseparable companions, navigating life's trials and triumphs together, always relying on the power of their unwavering eye contact to guide them. And in a world where words often fell short, they found solace in the depth of their shared gaze, a silent testament to their love and unbreakable bond.

In a quaint village nestled amidst a mysterious forest, there lived a young girl named Red Riding Hood. Unlike the tales often told, Red Riding Hood had an unexpected companion – a wise and gentle wolf who served as her guardian.In this reimagined story, the Wolf had long renounced his predatory ways, dedicating himself to protecting Little Red Riding Hood from the perils that lurked within the enigmatic Wood. With his keen senses and unwavering loyalty, he became her steadfast protector, guiding her through the treacherous forest.One fateful day, Red Riding Hood's mother tasked her with delivering a basket of goodies to her ailing grandmother, who resided on the other side of the Wood. Despite her trepidation, Red Riding Hood knew she could rely on her trusted guardian, the Wolf, to accompany her on this perilous journey.As they ventured into the Wood, the Wolf's instincts heightened, sensing potential dangers that lay ahead. He remained vigilant, keeping a watchful eye on his young charge. Red Riding Hood, reassured by his presence, skipped alongside him, her laughter echoing through the trees.Along their path, they encountered a sly Fox who attempted to deceive Red Riding Hood with his cunning words. But the Wolf, ever vigilant, intervened, exposing the Fox's trickery and ensuring Red Riding Hood's safety.Proceeding cautiously, they came across a sly Snake slithering through the undergrowth. With a swift and protective move, the Wolf intercepted the Snake, preventing it from harming Red Riding Hood. His unwavering dedication to her well-being remained steadfast.As they neared their destination, they stumbled upon a group of mischievous Squirrels who delighted in playing pranks on unsuspecting travelers. The Wolf, with his wisdom and resourcefulness, outsmarted the Squirrels, ensuring their journey remained uninterrupted.Finally, they arrived at Grandmother's cottage, where Red Riding Hood delivered the basket of goodies.

Nearly 80 miles of "smart motorway" will be created in the North West over the next five years as part of a £1.5 billion investment program in the region's roads. The scheme will see the M6, M60, M62 and M56 upgraded to "smart" motorways, using technology to monitor traffic and provide information to drivers. The work is expected to be completed by 2021. The Highways Agency said the investment would improve journey times, reduce congestion and boost the economy.

It was a monumental moment for the Mars colony when the first firearm was successfully 3D-printed on the red planet in the year 2050. The colonists had been working tirelessly to establish a self-sustaining community, and this achievement marked a significant step forward in their progress. As news of the accomplishment spread throughout the domed structures, excitement filled the air. The inhabitants of Mars had always believed in the importance of self-defense, even in their remote and isolated settlement. The firearm represented not only a means of protection but also a symbol of their ingenuity and resilience. The 3D printer, initially intended for manufacturing tools and equipment, had been reconfigured to produce a functional weapon. Its sleek design and ergonomic grip were a testament to the skill and creativity of the colonists. The engineer responsible for the breakthrough, Dr. Ava Patel, was hailed as a hero among the community. She had spent countless hours perfecting the printer's specifications and troubleshooting potential issues. With the successful printing of the firearm, the colonists felt a renewed sense of security in their Martian home. The weapon underwent extensive testing to ensure its reliability and safety. Martian law enforcement, consisting of a small but dedicated team, trained rigorously in firearm usage and maintenance. They were responsible for upholding the peace and preserving the integrity of the colony. As the sun set over the Martian horizon, casting a warm golden glow on the red landscape, the colonists gathered to celebrate their achievement. They shared stories, raised their cups in toasts, and expressed gratitude for their thriving community. The first shot fired rang out across the Martian plains, a symbolic gesture of their determination and progress. The firearm represented more than just a weapon; it was a testament to human adaptability and the indomitable spirit of exploration.

Original stars Ewan McGregor, Jonny Lee Miller, Ewan Bremner and Robert Carlyle are all returning for the new film.Speaking to The Hollywood Reporter, Boyle confirmed that the shoot was planned for "May/June".The film, loosely based on Irvine Welsh's Trainspotting follow-up Porno, could be released later next year.Boyle said filming of the sequel might be as tight as the first, which was shot in less than two months.Miller and Carlyle both star in US TV shows -  CBS's Elementary and ABC's Once Upon a Time respectively."They only get this little window off, so either you wait for the contract to run down, which could be as late as three years, or you kind of try and do it in this window," Boyle said."But all four lads are back in."The original Trainspotting was made on a shoestring budget of just Â£1.5m.The sequel's budget will be bigger but still relatively modest."We're doing it for less than $20m, and that'll give us control of the film so we can make the film we want to make," Boyle told the Hollywood Reporter."That's as much as you can get without being answerable to anybody. You can sort of get on with it without much interference. Although we could have raised a lot more money for this, we didn't, so we're trying to keep some sense like we kept the original."Boyle is hoping Film4, who produced the original, will get on board.He admitted there were reservations among the cast about making a sequel."The actors were understandably worried about its reputation and not wanting to let people down or to just be cashing in," he said."So actually the script, which John (Hodge) has written, has been put under a very fierce spotlight by everyone, and it's a good one."Everyone's attitude is that it'll be an honourable addition and an interesting addition to the original film. We're very positive that we believe it can happen and it's coming true."

Cameroon's Sebastien Siani has expressed his support for the use of video assistant referees (VAR) in football, following their deployment at the Confederations Cup. Siani, who played in Cameroon's 3-1 loss to Germany, witnessed firsthand the impact of VAR on the game, as referees used the technology to correct several key decisions. Despite some confusion and controversy, Siani believes that VAR will ultimately improve the sport. "I think VAR is a good thing for football," he said. "It helps the referees to make the right decisions and it's better for the game." Siani's sentiments echo those of Fifa president Gianni Infantino, who has described VAR as "the future of football."

The dying man tried to gather his thoughts, but his mind was a jumble of fear and confusion. He knew he didn't have much time left, and he wanted to make his last words count. He racked his brain, trying to think of something profound and meaningful to say, but everything he came up with seemed trite and hollow. He looked around the room, hoping to find inspiration in the faces of his loved ones, but they all seemed to be waiting for him to say something remarkable. The pressure was mounting, and the man could feel his time slipping away. In a moment of desperation, he blurted out a quote he had always loved, hoping it would sound wise and insightful, but it felt flat and unconvincing even to his own ears. As the last of his strength faded away, he could only hope that his words would be remembered, and that they would bring some comfort to those he left behind.

In the aftermath of the Great Cataclysm, the world lay in tatters. The libraries were among the first casualties, their invaluable collections reduced to ashes in the raging infernos that swept across the globe. In the absence of traditional textbooks, educators were forced to adapt, turning to the weathered notebooks of their teenage students as their new source of knowledge.Amidst the chaos, a resilient teacher named Mrs. Thompson stood resolute in her commitment to education. Her eyes shone with determination as she held aloft a tattered notebook, its pages filled with the scribbles and drawings of a bygone era.With passion in her voice, Mrs. Thompson began her lesson, breathing life into the stories and equations contained within those fragile pages. The teenagers, their hearts burdened by the weight of a shattered world, listened with rapt attention. In their eyes, a glimmer of hope ignited as they realized that knowledge could still be salvaged from the ruins.In a makeshift classroom, desks made from scavenged materials creaked under the weight of determination. The students, their faces etched with a mixture of curiosity and defiance, eagerly absorbed the wisdom imparted by their dedicated teacher.Though the pages of the notebooks were torn and stained, they held within them a precious legacy. Each notebook carried the dreams and aspirations of its former owner, now entrusted to a new generation tasked with rebuilding the world.As Mrs. Thompson flipped through the pages, the words and illustrations danced before the students' eyes. Stories of resilience and courage emerged from the depths of the notebooks, instilling within them a newfound sense of purpose.Through the faded ink and dog-eared pages, the teenagers discovered the beauty of poetry, the wonders of science, and the complexity of history. With each lesson, their minds expanded, and their spirits soared.

The Scottish SPCA said the owner could not longer look after the two large lizards, a female named Pintosh and a male called Babysha.They are being cared for at the charity's centre in Drumoak.Assistant manager Claire Tyczynska said: "We are rehoming them separately as they don't get along with one another."She explained: "As they are very large and powerful animals, they aren't suited to novice reptile owners and this is why we are specifically appealing to people who have kept iguanas before."Pintosh and Babysha are generally good natured with people, although they can be a little bit grumpy sometimes."Due to their size, they will each need a very large vivarium with plenty of space to move around."She added: "We would like to hear from anyone who feels they have the knowledge and experience to offer Pintosh or Babysha a new home."They will make fantastic pets for owners who will appreciate them and meet their care requirements."

A powerful explosion has killed at least 89 people in a restaurant in Petlawad, Madhya Pradesh, India. The blast is believed to have been caused by illegally stored mining explosives, which were being kept in a residential area. Police are searching for the shop owner, Rajendra Kasawa, who had a licence for the explosives but stored them illegally. The explosion also injured 39 people and caused significant damage to nearby buildings. The incident has sparked outrage and calls for stronger safety regulations to prevent such tragedies in the future.

I'd like to echo the advice of another commenter: do not pre-pay. Seriously, save yourself the trouble and just don't do it. Let me share with you the nightmare I went through when I made the mistake of pre-paying for a service that turned out to be a complete disaster. So, there I was, all excited and ready to get this service done. I had heard good things about the company, and their website looked professional enough. Little did I know that I was about to embark on a journey filled with frustration and regret. The first sign of trouble came when I arrived at the location on the scheduled day. The place was an absolute mess, like they weren't expecting anyone to show up. It was a bad omen, but I shrugged it off, thinking maybe they were just having a rough day. Oh boy, I was in for a rude awakening. As soon as I mentioned that I had pre-paid, their attitude changed completely. It was like I had just insulted their ancestors or something. The staff member at the counter gave me a condescending look and mumbled something under their breath. I couldn't believe it. I was treated like I didn't matter, like my business wasn't important to them. But I decided to give them the benefit of the doubt and proceeded with the service. Big mistake. The whole process was a disaster from start to finish. The staff members seemed completely clueless about what they were doing. They were fumbling around, making mistakes left and right, and had to keep calling for help. It was amateur hour at its finest. Not only was the service itself subpar, but the level of customer service I received was atrocious. The staff members were rude and dismissive, as if they couldn't be bothered to deal with my concerns. It was like they were doing me a favor by providing their so-called "service." At one point, I asked to speak to a manager to express my frustration. What a joke that was. The manager was just as unhelpful and dismissive as the rest of the staff.

It was time. We all took our places in the ritual circle underneath the starlit sky, while the old medicine lady chanted and sang in the middle. Each person eagerly awaited their transformation. As the moon reached its apex, slowly, everyone began to change. Skin grew hairier, teeth grew sharper, eyes grew wilder. Everyone began changing into the spirit animals they saw in their visions. Well, everyone except me, that is. As my friends changed into wolves, eagles, bears, snakes, they all saw that I retained my human form. A few laughed at me and mocked for being stuck in this pitiful existence. Some pitied me for not being able to experience the new freedom and strength that came with a new form. Others looked at me with revulsion, and considered me unworthy and unclean. One by one, as each one completed the transformation, they slipped into the night. The old lady shook her head at me and sighed, suggesting that perhaps I did n' t pray hard enough. Her eyes were filled with frustration and pity. Yes, I' ll try again next year; yes, I' ll bring a better offering; yes, I' ll pray more fervently; and all that stuff. I ca n' t say that I was too disappointed by my lack of transformation, though. I was n' t exactly distressed. I *am* a shapeshifter, after all.

Ayew, 26, signed for Swansea on a free transfer from Marseille in June 2015 and scored 12 goals in his first Premier League season.The Ghana international won the BBC African Player of the Year in 2011 and signs a four-year deal to become West Ham's record signing."The club have a lot of ambition and have a great manager," said Ayew."They want to become a bigger club every year and with the new stadium and a new training ground, you can feel they are moving on to another level and I want to be part of this project."He is the club's fifth signing this summer, as they prepare for their first season playing in the London Stadium.Sofiane Feghouli, Havard Nordtveit and Ashley Fletcher signed on free transfers from Valencia, Borussia Monchengladbach and Manchester United respectively.On Monday, the Hammers also completed the transfer of French left-back Arthur Masuaku from Olympiakos in a deal worth a reported £6m.The £20.5m fee for Ayew eclipses the £15m West Ham paid Liverpool for England striker Andy Carroll in June 2013.

Its unassuming exterior may fool you, but once you step inside, you'll be greeted with an atmosphere that exudes warmth and charm. The staff is incredibly friendly and attentive, making you feel like a cherished guest from the moment you walk in. The menu is a delightful mix of classic Italian dishes and innovative creations that showcase the chef's culinary prowess. I started my meal with the bruschetta, and it was a symphony of flavors with perfectly toasted bread, fresh tomatoes, and a drizzle of balsamic glaze. For the main course, I couldn't resist trying the homemade lasagna, and it was a revelation. Each layer was lovingly crafted, and the rich meat sauce combined with the gooey cheese was a match made in heaven. The portion was generous, leaving me satisfied and longing for leftovers. The dessert menu was equally impressive, and I couldn't resist the allure of the tiramisu. It was decadent, with layers of espresso-soaked ladyfingers and creamy mascarpone, creating a taste sensation that lingered on my palate. To accompany the meal, I indulged in a glass of Italian red wine recommended by the knowledgeable sommelier. The wine perfectly complemented the flavors of the dishes, enhancing the overall dining experience. The attention to detail in every aspect of the meal, from the presentation to the flavors, demonstrated the chef's dedication to culinary excellence. Without a doubt, this hidden gem deserves all the praise it receives and is a must-visit for anyone seeking an exceptional Italian dining experience.

This time, I decided to give their brunch a try on a lazy Sunday morning. The atmosphere was cozy with a mix of vintage furniture and quirky decorations. However, the service left much to be desired. It took ages for someone to acknowledge our presence, and even longer to bring us menus. Once we finally placed our orders, the wait for food seemed interminable. When the dishes did arrive, they were disappointingly lackluster. I opted for the avocado toast, which looked promising on the menu but fell flat on my plate. The bread was overly toasted and almost burnt, making it difficult to bite into. The avocado was not ripe enough and lacked the smooth, creamy texture I was hoping for. It felt like a haphazardly assembled dish rather than a carefully prepared one. My partner ordered the vegan scramble, which consisted of tofu and vegetables. While the flavors were decent, the portion size was underwhelming, leaving us both feeling unsatisfied. To make matters worse, the coffee was lukewarm and tasted stale. It seemed like it had been sitting in the pot for far too long. We tried to request a fresh pot, but the server was nowhere to be found. The lack of attentiveness from the staff was a recurring theme throughout the entire meal. As we glanced around the restaurant, it was evident that others were experiencing similar frustrations. Tables around us were growing impatient and the murmurs of discontent filled the air. It was clear that the establishment was understaffed and struggling to keep up with the demand. On top of everything, the prices were exorbitant considering the overall subpar experience. We expected a certain level of quality and service for the amount we were paying, but unfortunately, it was nowhere to be found. In the end, we left the Quiet Storm with empty stomachs and a sense of disappointment. It's a shame because we had heard positive things about this place, but our multiple visits have consistently left us unimpressed.

Food was way too average for the high price.

It's a shame it's the only real photography store in the city, but they could definitley do better. nnAs part of a black and white photography class, I needed to pick up film and photo paper, among a few other things. While Bernie's had everything I needed (and lots of other supplies) I didn't feel all that appreciated for coming in. I could tell he wanted to get to it quickly, but still felt like I was hassling the guy just being there. nnI hate to see real camera stores shut down, but unless I'm in a pinch I'm probably going to order supplies online from now on.

" It' s not your fault. You could n' t have known. " Those were the last words of the woman I called my enemy. The last words she said before she pulled the trigger, staining her red hair a deeper shade of red. I stared out of the window of the highrise building that held her office. Outside the building the fires burned, the people screamed, and the world called my name. How could I tell them that the same people that gave me my Suit were the ones that caused this destruction? I took my helmet off and dropped it to the floor, the heat was too much to handle. Her particular brand of anarchy could have saved us from this genocide, this extinction, if only I had n' t stopped her. I laugh at the irony, louder and louder until the tears flow down my face. Everything is wrong. I ca n' t get her last words out of my head. The last bit of kindness from a woman I wrote off as evil. I wonder if she knew what she would be doing to my mind when she said that. In the end it' s the perfect moment. The moment of closeness I never felt with another. Everything from her last words, the half smile, and the fact that the gun she left behind still had a few more bullets in it. " It' s not my fault. I could n' t have known. " I smile to the empty room, and wonder fleetingly if there will be anyone left to miss me.

Generating Entangled Photons from the Vacuum by Accelerated Measurements: Quantum Information Theory Meets the Unruh-Davies EffectAbstract:  We propose a novel scheme to generate polarization-entangled photon pairs from the vacuum by employing the Unruh-Davies effect. Our scheme is based on the idea of accelerated measurements, where an accelerating observer measures the polarization of a virtual photon pair created from the vacuum. We show that the accelerated measurements can induce entanglement between the two photons, even though they are separated by a space-like interval. Our scheme provides a new way to generate entangled photons, which may have applications in quantum information and communication.In this paper, we first introduce the Unruh-Davies effect and discuss its implications for quantum field theory. We then describe our scheme for generating entangled photons from the vacuum using accelerated measurements. We present the detailed calculations and show that our scheme can indeed generate entangled photons. Finally, we discuss the potential applications of our scheme in quantum information and communication.

The family patriarch ascended the grounds and steeled his gaze to the princely lord below. As he mounted his perch on the dewy lawn, a warm Spring breeze belied the shuddering forces soon to be unleashed... Eyes fixed on his hapless target, the elderly man prepared to explosively transform the potential to the kinetic. In his long fingers he rolled a hard, unyielding baseball. Like a practiced dancer, his right arm swung back overhead, the ball clenched tightly, split fingers aching to whip the object forward with as much thrust as he could muster. Eyes still fixed on the young boy below, nose down, a bead of sweat breaking on his brow, the force of a thousand lancers from Great Antiquity were called forth; his arm rocketed forward and released the projectile. It sailed over the grass like a cannon ball, dutifully obeying Newtonian physics with all the predictability and peril the Laws imply. As the cannon shot arched toward him, the young princely lord ran all his father' s training through his head in a torrent of words and memories. " Get in front of it. " " Keep your eye on the ball. " " Do n' t be afraid of it. " With scant moments to react, the boy placed himself in harm' s way. His leather glove fanned itself out like a flower. His knees bent slightly to prepare for the slightest adjustment in stance. His eyes remained locked on the target like a radar sweep onto a giant flying ship. The mighty missile arced across the Spring sky, momentarily blotting the sun. Gravity eventually won the day as the baseball loomed back toward Earth, its trajectory tailing into the form of small boy. With a mighty flinch, the boy asserted his place in the world. With elegance and skill he heaved his glove up and forward, snatching the sphere from the sky like a Nordic God. With a mighty WHUMP the ball lodged into the net of his glove, a tiny cloud of dust bursting forth from the impact. The smile of the victor crept upon the boy' s face. " Got it, Dad! "

This thesis provides a partial answer to a question posed by Greg Kuperbergin q-alg/9712003 and again by Justin Roberts as problem 12.18 in "Problems oninvariants of knots and 3-manifolds", math.GT/0406190, essentially: "Can onedescribe the category of representations of the quantum group U_q(sl_n)(thought of as a spherical category) via generators and relations?"  For each n \geq 0, I define a certain tensor category of trivalent graphs,modulo isotopy, and construct a functor from this category onto (a fullsubcategory of) the category of representations of the quantum group U_q(sl_n).One would like to describe completely the kernel of this functor, by providinggenerators. The resulting quotient of the diagrammatic category would then be acategory equivalent to the representation category of U_q(sl_n).  I make significant progress towards this, describing certain generators ofthe kernel, and some obstructions to further elements. It remains a conjecturethat these relations generate the kernel. My results extend those ofq-alg/9712003, MR1659228, math.QA/0310143 and math.GT/0506403.  The argument is essentially by constructing a diagrammatic version of theforgetful functor coming from the inclusion of U_q(sl_{n-1}) in U_q(sl_n}. Weknow this functor is faithful, so a diagram is in the kernel for n exactly ifits image under the diagrammatic forgetful functor is in the kernel for n-1.This allows us to perform inductive calculations, both establishing families ofelements of the kernel, and finding obstructions.

Tammy Beaumont is the only one of the 16-strong group not to have been part of last summer's defeat by Australia.Fellow batter Fran Wilson, who was in the Ashes Test squad, is not included despite recently being added to the list of centrally contracted players.All-rounder Laura Marsh also misses out from those involved in the Ashes.Seamer Natasha Farrant is the only other player from the 19 centrally contracted stars not chosen for this tour.Eight of the squad - including captain Charlotte Edwards - will join up with their team-mates a little over a week before the first one-day international because of their Women's Big Bash League (WBBL) commitments in Australia.England will play three one-day internationals, starting in Benoni on 7 February, before three Twenty20 matches against their South African counterparts."I can't wait to get going with the players in South Africa," said Robinson, who was appointed in November."Having started to work with some of them, having watched recent footage and through following the progress of those playing in the WBBL, there is no doubt that this is a hugely talented squad with enormous potential."Wicketkeeper Sarah Taylor is likely to win her 100th ODI cap on the tour, 10 years after she earned her first against India at Lord's.Charlotte Edwards (Kent, captain), Tammy Beaumont (Kent - T20s only), Katherine Brunt (Yorkshire), Kate Cross (Lancashire - ODIs only), Georgia Elwiss (Sussex), Lydia Greenway (Kent), Rebecca Grundy (Warwickshire), Jenny Gunn (Nottinghamshire), Danielle Hazell (Yorkshire), Amy Jones (Warwickshire, wk), Heather Knight (Berkshire, vice-captain), Natalie Sciver (Surrey), Anya Shrubsole (Somerset), Sarah Taylor (Sussex, wk), Lauren Winfield (Yorkshire), Danielle Wyatt(Nottinghamshire).

**Abstract**The interplay between electronic correlations and lattice distortions plays a crucial role in determining the ground state properties of many materials. In this work, we investigate the effects of electron-lattice coupling on charge order in the organic charge-transfer salt \theta-(ET)2X. We use a combination of theoretical and experimental techniques to demonstrate that the electron-lattice coupling leads to a significant enhancement of the charge order transition temperature and a reduction of the charge order gap. Our results provide new insights into the interplay between electronic correlations and lattice distortions in organic materials.

We report the detection of five Jovian mass planets orbiting high metallicitystars. Four of these stars were first observed as part of the N2K program andexhibited low RMS velocity scatter after three consecutive observations.However, follow-up observations over the last three years now reveal thepresence of longer period planets with orbital periods ranging from 21 days toa few years. HD 11506 is a G0V star with a planet of \msini = 4.74 \mjup in a3.85 year orbit. HD 17156 is a G0V star with a 3.12 \mjup planet in a 21.2 dayorbit. The eccentricity of this orbit is 0.67, one of the highest known for aplanet with a relatively short period. The orbital period for this planetplaces it in a region of parameter space where relatively few planets have beendetected. HD 125612 is a G3V star with a planet of \msini = 3.5 \mjup in a 1.4year orbit. HD 170469 is a G5IV star with a planet of \msini = 0.67 \mjup in a3.13 year orbit. HD 231701 is an F8V star with planet of 1.08 \mjup in a 142day orbit. All of these stars have supersolar metallicity. Three of the fivestars were observed photometrically but showed no evidence of brightnessvariability. A transit search conducted for HD 17156 was negative but coveredonly 25% of the search space and so is not conclusive.

I was bored, so I decided to browse the internet. I typed in "Fanfiction.net" and started browsing through the various stories. As I scrolled down the page, my eyes widened in horror. There were stories about me?! I couldn't believe it.I started reading one of the stories, my heart racing with anxiety. It was a dark and twisted tale about me, a fictional character, who had turned evil and was terrorizing the world. I couldn't believe what I was reading.I quickly opened another story, but it was even worse. This one had me as a villain, destroying cities and causing chaos wherever I went. I felt a chill run down my spine.I tried to shake off the feeling, telling myself it was just fiction. But the more I read, the more I became convinced that these stories were trying to tell me something.I started to feel like I was losing my grip on reality. Was I really a monster? Did I have some sort of evil alter ego that I didn't know about?I tried to shut off my computer, but my hands were shaking too much. I couldn't seem to pull myself away from the stories.As I continued to read, I started to notice a common theme. All of the stories had me meeting a tragic end. I would be defeated by a hero, or killed by a group of rebels.I felt a cold sweat on my brow. Was this my fate? Was I destined to be a villain, doomed to meet a tragic end?I couldn't take it anymore. I shut off my computer and pulled the plug out of the wall. I couldn't trust anything I read on that site.But even as I tried to shake off the feeling of dread, I couldn't shake the image of myself as a monster, terrorizing the world. It was like it was burned into my mind.I tried to distract myself by watching TV, but even the cheeriest shows couldn't lift my mood. I was haunted by the thought of those stories.Days passed and I couldn't shake off the feeling of unease. I avoided my computer, afraid of what I might find.But the image of myself as a monster lingered in my mind.

Pacquiao is being sued in Nevada after being accused of lying about the shoulder injury he suffered pre-fight.The Nevada Athletic Commission (NAC) says Pacquiao, 36, did not declare the problem, but the Filipino claims a treatment plan was agreed with United States Anti-Doping Agency (Usada).He could face a fine or suspension.Pacquiao is being sued by two people in Nevada who have alleged the boxer defrauded ticket buyers, television viewers and gamblers.They have opened lawsuits just hours after the NAC accused the fighter of being dishonest by not declaring his injury on a pre-fight questionnaire - which has also led to suggestions he could face a charge of perjury.The state attorney general's office is investigating.However, Pacquiao's adviser Michael Koncz said he took "full responsibility" for the "inadvertent mistake"."Number one, Manny didn't check the box," Koncz said. "I checked it. We weren't trying to hide anything. I just don't think I read the questionnaire correctly."Media playback is not supported on this devicePacquiao blamed his injury for his points defeat to undefeated Mayweather, 38, claiming it stopped him using his right hand.His camp claimed Usada had been notified of his treatment and plans for an anti-inflammatory shot on fight night.But Usada was only charged with monitoring anti-doping issues and NAC officials prevented the injection, saying they had not been told about Pacquaio's shoulder issue.No date has been set for surgery to repair the "significant tear" and although Pacquiao will face up to a year out, ESPN claims Mayweather is open to a re-match in 12 months time.Mayweather scored a unanimous points victory in the four-belt unification bout at the MGM Grand Garden Arena, the win extending the American's unbeaten record as a professional to 48 fights.He has said he intends to retire after one more fight, possibly in September, with Britain's Amir Khan among the potential opponents.

I sat alone in my apartment, staring at the walls and trying to make sense of the voice in my head. It had started a few days ago, out of nowhere, and at first, I thought it was just my imagination. But the voice was so clear, so real, that I couldn't shake the feeling that it was something more.It would tell me things, like what to wear in the morning and what to eat for breakfast. It would comment on my day, making snarky remarks about my job and my friends. And sometimes, it would just whisper strange things, like "You're not mad, but I'm pretty sure I am."I tried to ignore it, to convince myself that it was just a side effect of the stress and pressure of my daily life. But the voice only grew louder, more insistent. It was like having a constant companion, a shadow that followed me everywhere I went.One day, I decided to confront it. I sat down, took a deep breath, and said out loud, "Okay, voice in my head. What do you want from me?" There was a pause, and then it replied, "I want you to know that you're not crazy. You're just different."I shook my head, confused. "Different how?" I asked. "You'll see," the voice said, and then it was gone.I was left feeling relieved, but also a little scared. What did the voice mean? Was I really not crazy? And if not, then what was I? I didn't have the answers, but I knew that I would have to wait and see. The voice would reveal itself in time, and until then, I just had to trust that it was leading me somewhere.

By combining high-resolution (HST-WFPC2) and wide-field ground based (2.2mESO-WFI) and space (GALEX) observations, we have collected a multi-wavelengthphotometric data base (ranging from the far UV to the near infrared) of thegalactic globular cluster NGC1904 (M79). The sample covers the entire clusterextension, from the very central regions up to the tidal radius. In the presentpaper such a data set is used to study the BSS population and its radialdistribution. A total number of 39 bright ($m_{218}\le 19.5$) BSS has beendetected, and they have been found to be highly segregated in the cluster core.No significant upturn in the BSS frequency has been observed in the outskirtsof NGC 1904, in contrast to other clusters (M 3, 47 Tuc, NGC 6752, M 5) studiedwith the same technique. Such evidences, coupled with the large radius ofavoidance estimated for NGC 1904 ($r_{avoid}\sim 30$ core radii), indicate thatthe vast majority of the cluster heavy stars (binaries) has already sunk to thecore. Accordingly, extensive dynamical simulations suggest that BSS formed bymass transfer activity in primordial binaries evolving in isolation in thecluster outskirts represent only a negligible (0--10%) fraction of the overallpopulation.

I ca n' t see why I could n' t help.' Suicide is selfish.' That' s the part of this he never agreed with. When the pain has gotten so bad you' re unable to function, why do we look down on that? Is n' t it selfish to want someone to stick around, just so we are n' t sad that they' re gone? We ignore the constant pain they live with because we do n' t want to help them with their struggle, and we do n' t want to grieve their loss. He is n' t coming back. Not really. When nobody was paying attention, they dug chasms between each other and left him alone. We pretended it was n' t a problem he was gay, but we did n' t really notice how we acted. There were no invitations to paintball or hiking, and only gamed online. He would n' t go clothes shopping, or for girls night out. He was n' t a woman, but that' s how he got defined. We did n' t notice when he dropped off because we refused to see what was there. He' s not coming back; every time someone gets hurt because we assume they' re something they' re not and ignore them telling us otherwise, it' s the situation that made him. " I' ll be back soon enough " is n' t a promise. It' s a warning. If we do n' t take people as they are, we' ll lose them.

The distinction between genuine Blue Straggler stars (BSSs) and young stellar populations in dwarf spheroidal galaxies (dSphs) remains challenging. This study aims to disentangle the nature of the blue plume population in dSphs. Analyzing a sample of 9 non-star forming Local Group dwarf galaxies, we investigate the "BSS frequency" and compare it with that of Milky Way globular/open clusters and the halo. Our analysis reveals that the BSS frequency in dwarf galaxies, at a given absolute magnitude (Mv), consistently exceeds that of globular clusters with similar luminosities. Remarkably, the estimated BSS frequency in the lowest luminosity dSphs corresponds closely to observations in the Milky Way halo and open clusters. These findings suggest that the low-density, virtually collision-less environment of these dwarf galaxies indicates minimal dynamical evolution and insignificant production of collisional BSSs.In a follow-up investigation of two specific dSphs, Sculptor and Fornax, the presence of BSS candidates is examined. In Fornax, there are indications of contamination by young stars within the BSS population, while no clear evidence of recent star formation is found in Sculptor. The radial and luminosity distributions of BSS candidates are derived using wide field imaging data that extend beyond the nominal tidal radius of the galaxies. The radial distribution in Sculptor aligns with that of red horizontal branch (RHB) stars and agrees with theoretical expectations for BSSs formed through mass transfer in binaries. Conversely, the radial distribution of BSS candidates in Fornax is more concentrated compared to other considered stellar populations, supporting the hypothesis that a significant portion of BSS candidates in Fornax are indeed young stars. These findings are consistent with previous studies in the field.

I go to Sandcastle maybe once a year. But my Chicago friend Catherine came in to visit for the weekend and after a day at the zoo, we donned our swimsuits and came here. The main part of the park is closed in the evenings, but the Sandbar and the Sandbar Pool and the Hot Tub remain open for the 21 and over set. The Sandbar has a great menu of food and drinks and has a $6. 00 cover charge to get in. Occasionally there's live entertainment. nnIt's hard to believe that before they built this place, it was the site of an abandoned steel mill. Actually, it was the railroad yard for the nearby Homestead Works. But after site-clearing and clean-up, they turned it into a beautiful place to spend a warm day or nice evening.
